XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, AR XXXX Capital One EIN/TAX ID : XXXX Customer Service Department XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX UT XXXX XXXX : Formal Complaint Regarding Credit Card Denial Due to Past Account - Violation of FCRA Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally express my concern and lodge a complaint regarding the recent denial of my credit card application with Capital One. The adverse action notice I received stated that my application was denied due to a past account with Capital One. I believe this decision may be in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), which mandates that all credit reporting must be accurate, current, and relevant to the applicant 's present creditworthiness.

Details of the Denial : Date of Application : XX/XX/year> Reference Number : XXXX Violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) : The FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681, requires that credit information used to make credit decisions must be accurate, fair, and up-to-date. Denying my credit card application solely based on a past account, without considering my current creditworthiness or verifying whether the information is still relevant, is not consistent with the principles of the FCRA.

Moreover, the FCRA mandates that consumers have the right to dispute inaccurate or outdated information on their credit reports. If the denial was based on such information, I am requesting a re-evaluation of my application based on accurate and current data.

Request for Action : I respectfully request that Capital One : Reconsider my credit card application in light of my current financial status and credit history.
Provide a clear and detailed explanation of the specific reasons for the denial, as required by the FCRA.
Ensure that all information used in making this decision is accurate, relevant, and up-to-date.
Supporting Documentation : Enclosed with this letter are the following documents : A copy of the adverse action notice I received.
A copy of my credit report, highlighting any information related to the past account mentioned in the denial.
Please address this matter promptly. If I do not receive a satisfactory response, I may be compelled to file a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) or take legal action to ensure my rights under the FCRA are protected.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

narrative:
I am writing to dispute a decrease in my credit limit, which I believe was inaccurately implemented due to a letter sent out regrading the mistake as a result of a company merger from BB & T. This matter has come to my attention through a notification from your company, and I would like to clarify the circumstances surrounding this decision.

I have been a loyal customer of your company for several years, and I have always maintained a good standing with my credit account. However, I recently received a notification that my credit limit has been decreased, which has had a negative impact on my credit utilization ratio and overall credit score.

Upon further investigation, I discovered that the decrease in my credit limit was a result of a letter sent out mistakenly due to a company merger. This information was inaccurate and has unfairly affected my credit standing without any valid reason or basis. As a consumer, I have the right to accurate and transparent information regarding changes to my credit account, and I believe that this decrease was not justified.

I kindly request that you review the circumstances surrounding the inaccurate decrease in my credit limit and take the necessary steps to restore my original credit limit to the XXXX limit. I understand that company mergers can result in operational changes, but it is essential that these changes do not unfairly impact loyal customers like myself.

I expect a written response outlining the actions you will take to address this dispute and restore my credit limit back to its original status within 30 days. Failure to do so may result in further escalation of this matter to the appropriate regulatory authorities.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to a swift resolution and the restoration of my credit limit.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
I must bring to your attention that following my attempt to tender payment on the account in question, Capital One unilaterally closed my account without any prior notice or warning. I question how access to my own credit under the open-ended consumer credit plan, as defined in 15 U.S.C. 1602 ( g ), can be denied in such a manner. Moreover, Capital One has unjustly reported information to all three consumer reporting agencies without obtaining my written permissible purpose, in direct violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

Even if Capital One asserts that the terms and conditions permit such actions, I maintain that these terms are inherently fraudulent and deceptive due to the lack of proper disclosures that I never received under the Truth in Lending Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1605 ). Capital One has also failed to meet the requirements outlined in 15 U.S.C. 6801 and 6802, which protect my right to opt-out of having my information shared or reported to any third party.

By engaging in these practices, Capital One N.A. has not only harmed me as a consumer but has also failed to respond to the correspondence I previously sent. This has subjected me to what can only be described as wage and debt slavery, as the bank has deceptively and intentionally misappropriated the meanings of critical terms such as " payment, '' " promise to pay, '' and " pay, '' as defined in Black 's Law Dictionary, 4th Edition. According to this legal authority, " payment '' simply refers to the fulfillment of an obligation.

In our modern financial system, a " promise to pay '' has come to mean that collateral securities are created each time credits on the account are used, and these negotiable instruments are endorsed and utilized by banks and other financial institutions to access the Federal Reserves discount window. This process effectively tricks the consumer into believing that a debt exists, despite the absence of any actual gold, silver, or other materials of value as required by Article 1, Section 10 of the United States Constitution.

Given that Federal Reserve notes are themselves debt instruments, I must ask Capital One : if a debt is allegedly owed, why demand payment with another form of debt? Legally, a debt can only be " paid '' with money, not with another debt instrument. I am prepared to submit endorsed negotiable instruments as lawful tender of payment, in accordance with XXXX Article XXXX. These instruments will be stamped, signed, and endorsed to fulfill any supposed obligation attributed to XXXX XXXX XXXX, thus balancing Capital Ones public ledger.

It is Capital Ones responsibility, as the holder in due course, to apply the endorsed instrument as payment, earn interest from the collateral securities generated by my use of the card, and, if desired, redeem the instrument for Federal Reserve notes. This arrangement should ensure that I, as the beneficiary in this relationship, continue to have access to the card and benefit from higher credit lines and limits.

narrative:
I've been trying to use my 2 lounge access passes that they guaranteed came with my account when I opened my Venture card. They're supposed to expire at the end of XXXX but each time I try to access a lounge, I am turned down when they scan my card. I asked for any printed material, a letter, or anything I could present. They refused to provide anything. This means the access lounge access they promised when I signed up for the card is just a hoax.

They've given me different excuses. One time, they said it would take 14 days to get a membership number to provide me for the priority pass lounge. When I said OK, go ahead and start the request, they came around and said that's not actually the case. They said I only have access to the premium lounge. So then I asked for information on how to access a premium lounge ; they then came around and told me that I was not eligible.

This is clearly false advertising to lure people to sign up for the card. Now, the only option they are giving me is to upgrade to Venture X, which charges more annual fees. Consumers should be protected from this type of practice.

narrative: We were bumped off our original flight to XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. I re-tried to make another reservation but for a different hotel, XXXX XXXX XXXX since XXXX XXXX did not show up on the suggestions after I refreshed the app I was using. I used a different credit card this time to see if it would go through and it did. After the app showed my confirmed reservation with XXXX, the confirmation for XXXX XXXX also just came through. I called the app provider, XXXX and told them what happened. I asked to have the first reservation cancelled just minutes after I saw the reservation confirmation on their app. All of these were happening while our alternative flight was about to take off. Upon reaching XXXX, we checked in at the XXXX XXXX. I chatted with an XXXX represenative again to get an update on my cancellation with XXXX but I was told that since the terms of the reservation was non-refundable, the hotel was declining to refund us. I provided my chat history with XXXX and the email confirmation I received which reflected that the confirmation was an hour and a half later than the time and date that we were supposed to check-in to prove that I did not get the confirmation for the original hotel reservation on a timely manner but the card company sided with the hotel and now forcing me to pay for the rooms that we did not use. The hotel confirmed in the letter they provided with Discover that we didn't show and that the room was not occupied.

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
Goldman Sachs Bank USA XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Attn : Credit Services Department To whom Liable I am writing in response to an adverse action that has occurred regarding my credit account. Although my credit was extended through a digital signature, I have not received the consideration or the credit card that was supposedly issued to me. This is a serious issue that needs immediate rectification.

This letter serves as a **Qualified Written Request** under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ) 12 U.S.C. 2605 ( XXXX ), Regulation X at XXXX XXXX. XXXX, and the Truth-In-Lending-Act ( TILA ) XXXX ( XXXX ), 15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq., and 15 U.S.C. 1692 et seq., and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) 15 U.S.C. 1692g.

I request that you immediately open the credit card account that was extended to me via the digital signature. Failure to do so violates the contractual obligation that was made when my credit was extended. I demand that you either fulfill this obligation by providing the credit card and associated credit line or provide a detailed explanation as to why this has not occurred.

Furthermore, under 15 U.S.C. 6802 of the XXXX XXXX, I request information on any nonpublic personal information that may have been shared with third parties in connection with this situation.

In accordance with 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, I also request verification that no information related to identity theft has impacted this process. If such information is present, I request it be blocked and removed immediately.

Under 44 U.S.C. 3507 of the Paperwork Reduction Act, I request confirmation that any information collection related to my application complies with the necessary approval and review process mandated by the XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( XXXX ). Please provide the XXXX control number associated with any forms or procedures used during my application process, and confirm whether any information collected has been approved under this statute.

Please address this matter with the urgency it deserves. I expect immediate action to open my account as initially agreed upon.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

XXXX Purchased XXXX XXXX at IKEA and paid for their brand sponsored delivery service.

After items were received, and we attempted to assemble, there was damage to internal contents as well as missing parts.

XXXX All items returned to IKEA at the store a credit of {$610.00} was received. I opened case XXXX XXXXXXXX for the remaining {$41.00} to be refunded which was the delivery fee. It was not equitable to make a consumer pay for delivery of product that was damaged / missing product.

XX/XX/year> IKEA Customer Service issued a credit in the amount of {$41.00} to the card Comenity Bank aka Bread Financial has ZERO willingness to properly credit the account for this return.

The purchase was done on a 6-month promotional plan. That plan expires XX/XX/year>.

The account has other balances that are finance charge bearing. I understand that. However, it is absolutely inexcusable that a purchase, on a promotional 0 % interest be returned IN ITS ENTIRETY and the credit issued for RETURNING those items are applied to ANOTHER BALANCE in the account.

It will ABSOLUTELY be financially HARMFUL to me by this company refusing to properly credit the account. This company is TAKING ADVANTAGE of the consumer in a fiscally harmful manner.

Refusing to properly credit a return, will then FORCE the promotional balance to expire and INTEREST TO ACCRUE ON MERCHANDISE THAT IS NO LONGER IN CONSUMER POSSESSION!!!!

I have been trying to explain this problem via message service on customer card portal. I have not received any assistance. I was told to write a letter of complaint to Comenity Bank, and I doing that through the CFPB as I firmly believe a U.S. Government agency needs to be made aware this bank is not fairly treating its customers, and in fact costing customers money they should not be paying.

I am attaching the return receipt for {$610.00} from from return in store. I do not have a receipt for the {$41.00} credit as that was done by IKEA corporate customer care. This amount does show on my statement as a " CREDIT '' so it is clearly not a payment. I am also including my XXXX XXXXXX/XX/year> statement to show the two combined CREDITS are applied to an interest bearing balance THAT IS NOT RELATED TO THIS RETURN!!!

Simply put, if an item is returned THAT corresponding purchase on a consumer card should be credited. I have sincerely NEVER had this sort of crediting issue with any other merchant nor proprietary card carrier.

sub_issue: Credit card company isn't resolving a dispute about a purchase on your statement
narrative:
I disputed a charge on my Discover card, after a vendor continued to bill me, and make it literally, provably impossible for me to change my subscription status.
And invoice was sent to me with an email that did not work.
During the dispute, a phone number was provided, which also did not work.
Lastly, the merchant provided a process that is provably impossible to follow ( it is simply wrong ) to change my subscription status.
I have not been receiving service from this merchant ( XXXX ) for several months now, but they continue to bill me.

I opened a dispute with Discover. Surprisingly, Discovered maintained the positions that : 1 ) I must provide proof of cancellation, irrespective of it being impossible to obtain 2 ) It does not matter that the merchant has made it impossible 3 ) It does not matter that the merchant provided false information to Discover 4 ) Once I have made one purchase once, the merchant is entitled to bill my card forever, no matter what.

The merchant ( XXXX ) billed by credit card multiple times for a paid service.
The invoices were sent to an email which is not associated with the account.

The account itself is currently suspended and services are inaccessible, and XXXX has failed to respond to support requests for > 60 days. There is no way to contact support, no way to log in to change subscription status, no services being rendered.
While that issue in and of itself should be treated as a business fraud separate to this claim, the issue here is with Discover, who has set literally impossible requirements to challenge an incorrect charge on my card.

Due to their statement that the merchant may perpetually bill me forever for the service and there is nothing I can do about it, I had to close my account with Discover for my own protection.

Unfortunately, I can not easily capture the chat logs where the 4 positions of Discover listed above were laid out. In any request from the CFPB to Discover, the text of the chat logs should be requested.

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
My credit card provider ( Discover ) asked me to sign a 4056-C IRS form using their XXXX service , which I did and I have a confirmation email showing that I signed it within the requested 5 day period ( before XX/XX/XXXX ). I signed it on XX/XX/XXXX, I was not contacted about it again until XX/XX/XXXX, in an email in which they said they had not received the form...

However, the email said the following, verbatim : " Important : If you have already provided the consent requested and have not received any follow-up communication directly from Discover, there is no need for you to contact us. " ...

They indeed did not provide any other follow-up communication... given that I have no other emails, messages, or phone call attempts- and given that I had already provided the consent on the same day they sent it ( mind you, I have a confirmation email that I sent it )- I did not contact them as they explicitly said it wasn't necessary...

It is now XX/XX/XXXX, and I call in to their service line as I notice that I'm unable to use my credit card...

After being transferred a few times, they tell me they haven't received it and my account is pending closure ( because they apparently have no record of receiving the form ) - and will likely close and their is nothing they can do about it. They are going to report my account to the credit bureau even though I did everything they asked of me promptly. I ask to speak to the supervisor but she refuses telling me they have no process and she's the end of the line. After becoming a bit audibly frustrated ( I never cease to be cordial, albeit I did get a bit upset-sounding ) - she tells me " we're going around in circles '' and promptly disconnects the call.

I appreciate your immediate attention to this matter and expect a prompt resolution. Please contact me if you require any additional information or documentation from me.

Thank you for your cooperation in rectifying this issue in compliance with the consumer protection laws under 15 U.S. Code 1666d.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

narrative:
On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I went to PNC in XXXX, PA bank to withdrawal money from my account. The teller informed me that my PNC debit card expired and asked if I had another bank card which I did and gave it to her. She informed me that I could go see a bank associate ( XXXX XXXX at the desk and she could provide me with a new debit card. I went to the associate 's desk and gave her my license and expired debit card, she was on her computer for a moment and then told me she would be right back with my new card. She brought my new debit card to me and asked me if I would like her to shred my old card. I initially told her yes, then told her no, I would shred it at home. I took the cards, thanked her and left. This all took no more than 10 minutes or so.
On Tuesday, XX/XX/year> at XXXXXXXX XXXX, I received two notifications from XXXX ( via email and two through text ) that there were credit inquires but since XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I figured it could be with that and that I would review it when I had a chance.
On Wednesday, XX/XX/year> at XXXXXXXX XXXX, I received a call from PNC bank. I answered it and the woman stated she was calling me to review my credit inquiry. I immediately informed her that I was not looking for any credit and thaXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and I can't apply for credit which would affect my credit scores. I asked her who authorized it and she gave me a woman 's name ( XXXX XXXX and then I asked her who was the branch manager and she also provided me with the manager 's name ( XXXX XXXX. I informed this woman that I would be going to the bank first thing the next morning to speak to the branch manager.
On Thursday, XX/XX/year>, at XXXX XXXX, I went to the bank. There is a small table where bank associates sit and greet customers. I was asked how I can be helped and I informed the associate that I needed to see the branch manager. She asked why and then I noticed her nametag and it had XXXX on it and it was the same name I received the day prior. I asked her if she was the branch manager and she informed me no. Then I realized that she was the associate that took care of me Monday and I told her that she was the one who gave me my new debit card. She asked me what happened and asked me for my license and she looked me up on her laptop as I proceeded to tell her my story... asked her if the teller 's name was XXXX and she told me no and that her real name is XXXX but she goes by XXXX. I asked her how this could happen and then she looked at the computer and told me that she had in her notes that we discussed a credit card. I told her no, we did not have that discussion. She then proceeded to tell me that in her notes, she also has that we discussed a person loan. I told her she was lying and that those discussions never happened. I told her I know exactly how personal loans work, I received one the prior year from XXXX XXXX. I told her we had minimum conversation and that the video footage from the cameras need to be reviewed.
After about 30 minutes, I was able to meet with XXXX, the branch manager and also another associate, XXXX, in XXXX 's office. I explained to her what happened and asked the ladies how that could have happened to me. I was quite concerned about my credit for the new home and also identity theft. They didn't have any answers for me, there was speculation ; XXXX stated perhaps my profile was open when XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX met with the next customers. XXXX then proceeded to call a number regarding the credit card and informed them that it was put through in PNC 's error and that it needed to be suppressed and not show up on my credit card report. She called another number but that was an incorrect number and she told me that would need to call loss prevention regarding the personal loan. She informed me she would get back to me later that day, I left at XXXXXXXX XXXX, after being in the office for about 30 minutes.
I never heard back from XXXX but received a call from a PNC executive client relations associate regarding the matter. I was on the phone with her for about 30 minutes, informing her what happened and my concerns regarding my credit and identity theft. She informed me that she would be my point of contact going forward and that she wanted to set up a call the following Friday at XXXXXXXX XXXX  so that she could give me an update regarding the investigation, we did set that date and time.
On Friday, XX/XX/year>, at XXXX XXXX, I went to the local police department and reported the incident.
After that, I went back to the bank and met with XXXX and XXXX again. I asked them if there was any kind of signature, electronic and they told me no. XXXX said there was a loan number involved and that she could give that to me. She printed the paperwork that pertained to the credit card and also a one page sheet with the loan number on it. She explained thy do not need to get signatures during the application process. I told her that I didn't complete any application and she told me that XXXX did it. I informed them I went to the police and gave them the officer 's name and case number. I told them next steps for me was the CFPB and XXXX informed me that she already informed them about the credit card. I then asked her about the personal loan and she told me that she was working on that. I thanked them for their time and left.
Monday, I left a message the executive client relations associate and she called me back on Tuesday and I gave her an update. She told me that the CFPB is for customers to use and that it isn't for staff to report complaints on customers ' behalf. She also informed me that there is a different branch in her division that handles complaints that come through CFPB so this shows that it was not reported like the branch manager indicated.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, AR XXXX Capital One EIN/TAX ID : XXXX Customer Service Department XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX UT XXXX XXXX : Formal Complaint Regarding Credit Card Denial Due to Past Account - Violation of FCRA Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally express my concern and lodge a complaint regarding the recent denial of my credit card application with Capital One. The adverse action notice I received stated that my application was denied due to a past account with Capital One. I believe this decision may be in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), which mandates that all credit reporting must be accurate, current, and relevant to the applicant 's present creditworthiness.

Details of the Denial : Date of Application : XX/XX/year> Reference Number : XXXX Violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) : The FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681, requires that credit information used to make credit decisions must be accurate, fair, and up-to-date. Denying my credit card application solely based on a past account, without considering my current creditworthiness or verifying whether the information is still relevant, is not consistent with the principles of the FCRA.

Moreover, the FCRA mandates that consumers have the right to dispute inaccurate or outdated information on their credit reports. If the denial was based on such information, I am requesting a re-evaluation of my application based on accurate and current data.

Request for Action : I respectfully request that Capital One : Reconsider my credit card application in light of my current financial status and credit history.
Provide a clear and detailed explanation of the specific reasons for the denial, as required by the FCRA.
Ensure that all information used in making this decision is accurate, relevant, and up-to-date.
Supporting Documentation : Enclosed with this letter are the following documents : A copy of the adverse action notice I received.
A copy of my credit report, highlighting any information related to the past account mentioned in the denial.
Please address this matter promptly. If I do not receive a satisfactory response, I may be compelled to file a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) or take legal action to ensure my rights under the FCRA are protected.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
sub_issue: Credit card company won't increase or decrease your credit limit
narrative:
I am writing to dispute a decrease in my credit limit, which I believe was inaccurately implemented due to a letter sent out regrading the mistake as a result of a company merger from BB & T. This matter has come to my attention through a notification from your company, and I would like to clarify the circumstances surrounding this decision.

I have been a loyal customer of your company for several years, and I have always maintained a good standing with my credit account. However, I recently received a notification that my credit limit has been decreased, which has had a negative impact on my credit utilization ratio and overall credit score.

Upon further investigation, I discovered that the decrease in my credit limit was a result of a letter sent out mistakenly due to a company merger. This information was inaccurate and has unfairly affected my credit standing without any valid reason or basis. As a consumer, I have the right to accurate and transparent information regarding changes to my credit account, and I believe that this decrease was not justified.

I kindly request that you review the circumstances surrounding the inaccurate decrease in my credit limit and take the necessary steps to restore my original credit limit to the XXXX limit. I understand that company mergers can result in operational changes, but it is essential that these changes do not unfairly impact loyal customers like myself.

I expect a written response outlining the actions you will take to address this dispute and restore my credit limit back to its original status within 30 days. Failure to do so may result in further escalation of this matter to the appropriate regulatory authorities.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to a swift resolution and the restoration of my credit limit.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
I must bring to your attention that following my attempt to tender payment on the account in question, Capital One unilaterally closed my account without any prior notice or warning. I question how access to my own credit under the open-ended consumer credit plan, as defined in 15 U.S.C. 1602 ( g ), can be denied in such a manner. Moreover, Capital One has unjustly reported information to all three consumer reporting agencies without obtaining my written permissible purpose, in direct violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

Even if Capital One asserts that the terms and conditions permit such actions, I maintain that these terms are inherently fraudulent and deceptive due to the lack of proper disclosures that I never received under the Truth in Lending Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1605 ). Capital One has also failed to meet the requirements outlined in 15 U.S.C. 6801 and 6802, which protect my right to opt-out of having my information shared or reported to any third party.

By engaging in these practices, Capital One N.A. has not only harmed me as a consumer but has also failed to respond to the correspondence I previously sent. This has subjected me to what can only be described as wage and debt slavery, as the bank has deceptively and intentionally misappropriated the meanings of critical terms such as " payment, '' " promise to pay, '' and " pay, '' as defined in Black 's Law Dictionary, 4th Edition. According to this legal authority, " payment '' simply refers to the fulfillment of an obligation.

In our modern financial system, a " promise to pay '' has come to mean that collateral securities are created each time credits on the account are used, and these negotiable instruments are endorsed and utilized by banks and other financial institutions to access the Federal Reserves discount window. This process effectively tricks the consumer into believing that a debt exists, despite the absence of any actual gold, silver, or other materials of value as required by Article 1, Section 10 of the United States Constitution.

Given that Federal Reserve notes are themselves debt instruments, I must ask Capital One : if a debt is allegedly owed, why demand payment with another form of debt? Legally, a debt can only be " paid '' with money, not with another debt instrument. I am prepared to submit endorsed negotiable instruments as lawful tender of payment, in accordance with XXXX Article XXXX. These instruments will be stamped, signed, and endorsed to fulfill any supposed obligation attributed to XXXX XXXX XXXX, thus balancing Capital Ones public ledger.

It is Capital Ones responsibility, as the holder in due course, to apply the endorsed instrument as payment, earn interest from the collateral securities generated by my use of the card, and, if desired, redeem the instrument for Federal Reserve notes. This arrangement should ensure that I, as the beneficiary in this relationship, continue to have access to the card and benefit from higher credit lines and limits.

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
My credit card provider ( Discover ) asked me to sign a 4056-C IRS form using their XXXX service , which I did and I have a confirmation email showing that I signed it within the requested 5 day period ( before XX/XX/XXXX ). I signed it on XX/XX/XXXX, I was not contacted about it again until XX/XX/XXXX, in an email in which they said they had not received the form...

However, the email said the following, verbatim : " Important : If you have already provided the consent requested and have not received any follow-up communication directly from Discover, there is no need for you to contact us. " ...

They indeed did not provide any other follow-up communication... given that I have no other emails, messages, or phone call attempts- and given that I had already provided the consent on the same day they sent it ( mind you, I have a confirmation email that I sent it )- I did not contact them as they explicitly said it wasn't necessary...

It is now XX/XX/XXXX, and I call in to their service line as I notice that I'm unable to use my credit card...

After being transferred a few times, they tell me they haven't received it and my account is pending closure ( because they apparently have no record of receiving the form ) - and will likely close and their is nothing they can do about it. They are going to report my account to the credit bureau even though I did everything they asked of me promptly. I ask to speak to the supervisor but she refuses telling me they have no process and she's the end of the line. After becoming a bit audibly frustrated ( I never cease to be cordial, albeit I did get a bit upset-sounding ) - she tells me " we're going around in circles '' and promptly disconnects the call.

Certificates of Indebtedness : There has been no transparent communication or presentation of documents regarding the handling of my certificates of indebtedness securities. This lack of disclosure and clarity raises serious concerns about the management and reporting of my financial activities.

I am writing to dispute the reduction in my credit limit that was recently imposed by your company. The decision to decrease my credit limit is in violation of the protections provided under the 15 U.S. Code 1666d, which safeguards consumers against arbitrary credit limit reductions based on inaccurate information.

Upon reviewing my credit report and account history, I have identified inaccuracies that have led to the reduction in my credit limit. It appears that there are errors in the reporting of my payment history and outstanding balances, which have unfairly impacted my credit standing and access to credit.

As per the regulations outlined in the 15 U.S. Code 1666d, I urge you to conduct a thorough investigation into the information on my credit report and account history. It is imperative that these discrepancies are corrected to accurately reflect my financial standing and creditworthiness.

I request that you review my account and credit limit in accordance with the provisions of the 15 U.S. Code 1666d and take the necessary steps to restore my credit limit to its previous level. I have always maintained a good credit history and have abided by the terms of my credit agreement.

I appreciate your immediate attention to this matter and expect a prompt resolution. Please contact me if you require any additional information or documentation from me.

Thank you for your cooperation in rectifying this issue in compliance with the consumer protection laws under 15 U.S. Code 1666d.

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

narrative:
I looked at my Citibank Double Cash card on XX/XX/ 2024, and I found a long list of unauthorized charges beginning on XX/XX/XXXX, total up to between {$1000.00} - {$1600.00}. I logged into my Citibank app and reported my card lost/stolen immediately, and flagged all the fraudulent charges to Citibank. Because some of the charges were still pending and did not have a dispute charge button for me to click on, I called into Citibanks fraud department on Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX PST and spoke to a fraud specialist to ensure that all unauthorized charges would be marked as fraudulent. As I was waiting for their decision, Citibanks fraud department called me back and asked me a series of questions again about my charges ( I do not remember the date of this call ). The fraud specialist ensured that I would not be liable for any of charges during the investigation period. Today, XX/XX/2024 at XXXX PST, I checked for an update on my fraud charges, only to see that Citibank decided that I would be liable for almost every single unauthorized charge ( a few more charges are still under investigation ). I called XXXX to protest this decision, but the fraud specialist said that the appeals department opens at XXXX PST, and I would need to call back then.

I am beyond upset with Citibanks decision because I do not think they did a proper investigation of these fraudulent charges on my credit card. I lost my physical card in XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX on XX/XX/2024. When I click on the fraudulent transactions within the app, almost all transactions were purchased in-store in XXXX XXXX XXXX between XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/2024. sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
sub_issue: Card opened without my consent or knowledge
narrative:
On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I went to PNC in XXXX, PA bank to withdrawal money from my account. The teller informed me that my PNC debit card expired and asked if I had another bank card which I did and gave it to her. She informed me that I could go see a bank associate ( XXXX XXXX at the desk and she could provide me with a new debit card. I went to the associate 's desk and gave her my license and expired debit card, she was on her computer for a moment and then told me she would be right back with my new card. She brought my new debit card to me and asked me if I would like her to shred my old card. I initially told her yes, then told her no, I would shred it at home. I took the cards, thanked her and left. This all took no more than 10 minutes or so.
On Tuesday, XX/XX/year> at XXXXXXXX XXXX, I received two notifications from XXXX ( via email and two through text ) that there were credit inquires but since XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I figured it could be with that and that I would review it when I had a chance.
On Wednesday, XX/XX/year> at XXXXXXXX XXXX, I received a call from PNC bank. I answered it and the woman stated she was calling me to review my credit inquiry. I immediately informed her that I was not looking for any credit and thaXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and I can't apply for credit which would affect my credit scores. I asked her who authorized it and she gave me a woman 's name ( XXXX XXXX and then I asked her who was the branch manager and she also provided me with the manager 's name ( XXXX XXXX. I informed this woman that I would be going to the bank first thing the next morning to speak to the branch manager.
On Thursday, XX/XX/year>, at XXXX XXXX, I went to the bank. There is a small table where bank associates sit and greet customers. I was asked how I can be helped and I informed the associate that I needed to see the branch manager. She asked why and then I noticed her nametag and it had XXXX on it and it was the same name I received the day prior. I asked her if she was the branch manager and she informed me no. Then I realized that she was the associate that took care of me Monday and I told her that she was the one who gave me my new debit card. She asked me what happened and asked me for my license and she looked me up on her laptop as I proceeded to tell her my story... asked her if the teller 's name was XXXX and she told me no and that her real name is XXXX but she goes by XXXX. I asked her how this could happen and then she looked at the computer and told me that she had in her notes that we discussed a credit card. I told her no, we did not have that discussion. She then proceeded to tell me that in her notes, she also has that we discussed a person loan. I told her she was lying and that those discussions never happened. I told her I know exactly how personal loans work, I received one the prior year from XXXX XXXX. I told her we had minimum conversation and that the video footage from the cameras need to be reviewed.
After about 30 minutes, I was able to meet with XXXX, the branch manager and also another associate, XXXX, in XXXX 's office. I explained to her what happened and asked the ladies how that could have happened to me. I was quite concerned about my credit for the new home and also identity theft. They didn't have any answers for me, there was speculation ; XXXX stated perhaps my profile was open when XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX met with the next customers. XXXX then proceeded to call a number regarding the credit card and informed them that it was put through in PNC 's error and that it needed to be suppressed and not show up on my credit card report. She called another number but that was an incorrect number and she told me that would need to call loss prevention regarding the personal loan. She informed me she would get back to me later that day, I left at XXXXXXXX XXXX, after being in the office for about 30 minutes.
I never heard back from XXXX but received a call from a PNC executive client relations associate regarding the matter. I was on the phone with her for about 30 minutes, informing her what happened and my concerns regarding my credit and identity theft. She informed me that she would be my point of contact going forward and that she wanted to set up a call the following Friday at XXXXXXXX XXXX  so that she could give me an update regarding the investigation, we did set that date and time.
On Friday, XX/XX/year>, at XXXX XXXX, I went to the local police department and reported the incident.
After that, I went back to the bank and met with XXXX and XXXX again. I asked them if there was any kind of signature, electronic and they told me no. XXXX said there was a loan number involved and that she could give that to me. She printed the paperwork that pertained to the credit card and also a one page sheet with the loan number on it. She explained thy do not need to get signatures during the application process. I told her that I didn't complete any application and she told me that XXXX did it. I informed them I went to the police and gave them the officer 's name and case number. I told them next steps for me was the CFPB and XXXX informed me that she already informed them about the credit card. I then asked her about the personal loan and she told me that she was working on that. I thanked them for their time and left.
Monday, I left a message the executive client relations associate and she called me back on Tuesday and I gave her an update. She told me that the CFPB is for customers to use and that it isn't for staff to report complaints on customers ' behalf. She also informed me that there is a different branch in her division that handles complaints that come through CFPB so this shows that it was not reported like the branch manager indicated.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, AR XXXX Capital One EIN/TAX ID : XXXX Customer Service Department XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX UT XXXX XXXX : Formal Complaint Regarding Credit Card Denial Due to Past Account - Violation of FCRA Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally express my concern and lodge a complaint regarding the recent denial of my credit card application with Capital One. The adverse action notice I received stated that my application was denied due to a past account with Capital One. I believe this decision may be in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), which mandates that all credit reporting must be accurate, current, and relevant to the applicant 's present creditworthiness.

Details of the Denial : Date of Application : XX/XX/year> Reference Number : XXXX Violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) : The FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681, requires that credit information used to make credit decisions must be accurate, fair, and up-to-date. Denying my credit card application solely based on a past account, without considering my current creditworthiness or verifying whether the information is still relevant, is not consistent with the principles of the FCRA.

sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
narrative:
I must bring to your attention that following my attempt to tender payment on the account in question, Capital One unilaterally closed my account without any prior notice or warning. I question how access to my own credit under the open-ended consumer credit plan, as defined in 15 U.S.C. 1602 ( g ), can be denied in such a manner. Moreover, Capital One has unjustly reported information to all three consumer reporting agencies without obtaining my written permissible purpose, in direct violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

Even if Capital One asserts that the terms and conditions permit such actions, I maintain that these terms are inherently fraudulent and deceptive due to the lack of proper disclosures that I never received under the Truth in Lending Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1605 ). Capital One has also failed to meet the requirements outlined in 15 U.S.C. 6801 and 6802, which protect my right to opt-out of having my information shared or reported to any third party.

By engaging in these practices, Capital One N.A. has not only harmed me as a consumer but has also failed to respond to the correspondence I previously sent. This has subjected me to what can only be described as wage and debt slavery, as the bank has deceptively and intentionally misappropriated the meanings of critical terms such as " payment, '' " promise to pay, '' and " pay, '' as defined in Black 's Law Dictionary, 4th Edition. According to this legal authority, " payment '' simply refers to the fulfillment of an obligation.

In our modern financial system, a " promise to pay '' has come to mean that collateral securities are created each time credits on the account are used, and these negotiable instruments are endorsed and utilized by banks and other financial institutions to access the Federal Reserves discount window. This process effectively tricks the consumer into believing that a debt exists, despite the absence of any actual gold, silver, or other materials of value as required by Article 1, Section 10 of the United States Constitution.

Given that Federal Reserve notes are themselves debt instruments, I must ask Capital One : if a debt is allegedly owed, why demand payment with another form of debt? Legally, a debt can only be " paid '' with money, not with another debt instrument. I am prepared to submit endorsed negotiable instruments as lawful tender of payment, in accordance with XXXX Article XXXX. These instruments will be stamped, signed, and endorsed to fulfill any supposed obligation attributed to XXXX XXXX XXXX, thus balancing Capital Ones public ledger.

It is Capital Ones responsibility, as the holder in due course, to apply the endorsed instrument as payment, earn interest from the collateral securities generated by my use of the card, and, if desired, redeem the instrument for Federal Reserve notes. This arrangement should ensure that I, as the beneficiary in this relationship, continue to have access to the card and benefit from higher credit lines and limits.

During the dispute, a phone number was provided, which also did not work.

I have not been receiving service from this merchant ( XXXX ) for several months now, but they continue to bill me.

I opened a dispute with Discover. Surprisingly, Discovered maintained the positions that : 1 ) I must provide proof of cancellation, irrespective of it being impossible to obtain 2 ) It does not matter that the merchant has made it impossible 3 ) It does not matter that the merchant provided false information to Discover 4 ) Once I have made one purchase once, the merchant is entitled to bill my card forever, no matter what.

The merchant ( XXXX ) billed by credit card multiple times for a paid service.
The invoices were sent to an email which is not associated with the account.

The account itself is currently suspended and services are inaccessible, and XXXX has failed to respond to support requests for > 60 days. There is no way to contact support, no way to log in to change subscription status, no services being rendered.
While that issue in and of itself should be treated as a business fraud separate to this claim, the issue here is with Discover, who has set literally impossible requirements to challenge an incorrect charge on my card.

Due to their statement that the merchant may perpetually bill me forever for the service and there is nothing I can do about it, I had to close my account with Discover for my own protection.

Unfortunately, I can not easily capture the chat logs where the 4 positions of Discover listed above were laid out. In any request from the CFPB to Discover, the text of the chat logs should be requested.

After becoming a bit audibly frustrated ( I never cease to be cordial, albeit I did get a bit upset-sounding ) - she tells me " we're going around in circles '' and promptly disconnects the call.

I am writing to file a formal complaint against Wells Fargo regarding their inadequate response to my claims of fraudulent activity on my banking account, as well as their refusal to acknowledge my situation as a victim of a robbery and a data breach.

Recently, I received correspondence from Wells Fargo stating, " Thank you for contacting us. We completed our research and are providing details to address your concerns. Unfortunately, your request for the bank to write letters to your creditors explaining why payments were returned from your checking account ending in XXXX can not be fulfilled. '' The letter further indicated that they failed to provide any new insights or information regarding my case, as my concerns were considered resolved. However, the information they provided is inaccurate and does not appropriately address the severity of my situation.

I have previously submitted police reports documenting my robbery, along with evidence of fraudulent charges on my banking account. I clearly highlighted these transactions in my bank statements, and yet Wells Fargo has chosen to overlook this information while stating that they would not write letters to my creditors regarding my returned payments. This disregard for my circumstances is not only frustrating but also detrimental to my financial well-being.

Moreover, Wells Fargo 's approach to handling my complaint has been confusing and unsatisfactory. They seem to maintain that my concerns have been addressed while dismissing the clear evidence I provided. I believe their response lacks the seriousness it deserves, especially considering that I am a victim in this case and that my credit history has been negatively impacted due to these unresolved issues.

In light of these circumstances, I urge the CFPB to assist in investigating Wells Fargo 's response and practices regarding cases of fraud and customer complaints. It is essential that victims of robbery and data breaches receive adequate support and fair treatment, rather than receiving dismissive responses.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your assistance in addressing my concerns regarding Wells Fargo.

Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding a significant financial loss I have incurred due to a fraudulent investment scheme and the subsequent lack of resolution from both the involved financial institutions and regulatory bodies.

On XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was deceived into participating in an investment scheme orchestrated by individuals posing as representatives of XXXX XXXX XXXX. The fraudster, identifying himself as " XXXX, '' contacted me via text message, claiming that there was a fictitious charge on my account and that my IP and banking information had been compromised. He provided a phone number ( XXXX XXXX ) for further communication.

Under the guise of protecting my assets, I was coerced into making a Wire transfer payment from my Citizens Bank account. This transaction resulted in a total loss of {$25000.00}. Despite partial recoveries facilitated by a scam recovery service named XXXX XXXX, I am still facing substantial financial detriment.

After discovering the scam, I immediately contacted Citizens Bank to report the fraudulent activity and requested a reversal of the transaction. The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ) intervened and facilitated communication with Citizens Bank. Although Citizens Bank initially indicated they would assist, there has been no substantive progress towards resolving the issue or providing a refund.

Despite multiple communications via email and phone, Citizens Bank has failed to provide any resolution or refund for the fraudulent transaction.

Given the lack of response and effective action from Citizens Bank following OCC 's involvement, I am now turning to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) for assistance. Enclosed with this letter are copies of the following documents to support my complaint : 1. Letter to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ) detailing the fraudulent activities and Citizens Bank 's response.

2. Correspondence with Citizens Bank indicating their initial commitment to assist.

3. Bank statements showing the unauthorized withdrawal and transaction.

I respectfully request the CFPB to : 1. Conduct a thorough investigation into the fraudulent activities and Citizens Bank 's failure to address the issue adequately despite OCC 's intervention.

2. Facilitate the recovery of the lost funds from Citizens Bank.

3. Implement measures to prevent similar scams from affecting other consumers in the future.

I am available to provide any additional information or clarification as needed. Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated. I look forward to your response and the resolution of this serious issue.

Thank you for your assistance.

Complaint Submission : Issue with Truist Bank Regarding {$500.00} Payment and Lack of Communication To : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Regarding : Truist Bank **Date : XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX  Subject : Truist Banks Mishandling of a {$500.00} Payment to XXXX  XXXX, Resulting in Financial Harm Due to Lack of Communication and Error Details : I am filing a formal complaint against Truist Bank due to their mishandling of a {$500.00} payment I attempted to make to my XXXX XXXXXXXX account, which resulted in financial harm and potential negative impact on my creditworthiness. Below is a detailed account of the events and issues.

1. Timeline of Events : - On XXXX XXXX XXXX I attempted to make a {$500.00} payment from my Truist checking account to my XXXX XXXX credit card account.
- Upon submitting the payment, I observed that the {$500.00} was deducted from my available balance. My account balance showed that I had approximately {$0.00} left, indicating that the payment was processed.
- I was under the impression that the payment had been successfully initiated and was on track to be credited to my XXXX XXXX account.

2. Issue with Transaction Posting : - A few days later, I checked my Truist account and noticed that the {$500.00} payment was no longer reflected in my transaction history. It had completely disappeared from my recent transactions list, and the balance no longer showed that the {$500.00} had been deducted.
- This disappearance led me to question what had happened to the payment, as I never received any notification from Truist that the payment was declined, reversed, or had bounced. I assumed that since the money had left my account and I had the funds available at the time, the payment would have gone through.

3. Truist 's Explanation and Lack of Communication : - After contacting Truist Bank to inquire about the missing {$500.00} payment, I was told by a representative that the payment had not actually left my account and that it had been declined due to insufficient funds. According to the representative, the bank used its discretion to avoid an overdraft situation because my account was short by {$0.00} at the time of the payment processing.
- The representative further explained that the {$500.00} did not go through because there was a separate pending transaction from an earlier purchase, which included a tip. However, this explanation does not align with my understanding or observations. When I initially made the {$500.00} payment, the funds were in my account, and the {$500.00} had already been deducted from my balance. I was not informed at any point that the funds were insufficient or that the payment was being declined due to this separate transaction.

4. Lack of Notification and Financial Harm : - At no point did Truist notify me that the {$500.00} payment had been declined or that my account had insufficient funds. This lack of communication caused significant confusion and led me to believe that the payment had been successfully processed.
- I was not given any opportunity to address the alleged shortfall of {$0.00} because Truist failed to inform me of the issue. As a result, the payment to XXXX XXXX did not go through, and I was left with the false belief that my XXXX XXXX account had been credited with the {$500.00}.
- Truist also failed to notify XXXX XXXX that the payment had bounced or was declined. This lack of communication between the banks has resulted in further complications for me, as my XXXX XXXX account was never credited, and I am now at risk of late payment fees and damage to my credit score due to a payment that I attempted to make in good faith.

5. Discrepancy Between Account Balance and Payment : - I have consistently informed Truist representatives that I saw the {$500.00} leave my account, and my balance reflected this deduction. Truists claim that the money never left the account does not match what I observed. There is clearly a discrepancy between what was shown in my account and what Truist is now claiming.
- If Truist had properly informed me that there was a problem with the payment, I could have resolved the issue immediately by transferring additional funds to cover the {$0.00} shortfall. Instead, their failure to notify me has caused financial hardship, stress, and confusion.

6. Financial and Credit Impact : - The failure to process the {$500.00} payment has directly impacted my ability to pay my XXXX XXXX bill, potentially leading to late fees, penalties, and damage to my credit score. This could have been entirely avoided if Truist had communicated with me promptly and accurately.
- Additionally, I am concerned that this error may affect my ability to access credit in the future due to potential negative marks on my credit report. This situation has caused me financial harm through no fault of my own.

End Conclusion : Truist Banks failure to notify me of the declined payment, coupled with the disappearance of the {$500.00} transaction from my account history, has caused significant financial harm and stress. I acted in good faith, believing that I had successfully made the payment, and Truists error has led to complications that are not my fault. I request that the CFPB investigate this issue and ensure that Truist takes appropriate action to resolve it.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I appreciate your assistance in resolving this issue.

XXXX XXXX XXXX Ally Bank is negligent of not investigating previously canceled recurring XXXX transfers that reappear several months after they were canceled and result in unauthorized attempts to make withdrawals from customers accounts. In addition, Ally Banks record keeping practices regarding customers requests to schedule, modify, and cancel recurring XXXX transfers are weak as they allow deletion of the information.

Dear CFPB, I am writing to express my deep concern regarding an attempt of Ally Bank to transfer funds in amount of {$2700.00} from my checking account on XX/XX/XXXX, which Ally Bank claims were part of a recurring monthly XXXX transfer. This incident is troubling given the fact that I had explicitly canceled the entire recurring transfer series in question on XX/XX/XXXX.

On XX/XX/XXXX I had scheduled a recurring monthly XXXX transfer in the amount of {$2700.00} from my checking account ending in XXXX to one of my XXXX recipients XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) at XXXX with the initial transfer anticipated to occur on XX/XX/XXXX and then on the XXXX of every subsequent month ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, etc ). However, subsequently I canceled the entire series of the recurring transfers on XX/XX/XXXX. In addition, in XX/XX/XXXX, I canceled absolutely all remaining ( I had at least three other recurring XXXX transfers with Ally Bank at that time ) recurring transfers associated with my account. At the end of XX/XX/XXXX I also verified through my online banking that no outstanding future scheduled XXXX transfers were showing up on my account.

Despite these clear cancellations, there was an unexpected attempt to make a XXXX transfer from my account on XX/XX/XXXX for the amount matching the previously canceled recurring transfer. When I contacted Ally Bank and learned that my account was blocked due to not enough funds to cover the transfer I informed Ally Bank that the transfer attempt was clearly a banks error since I had canceled all recurring transfers months prior. The Ally Banks representative I was conversing with acknowledged the cancellation I had made in XXXX and indicated that they would file a report ( case number XXXX ) so that Ally Bank would investigate the matter.

However, my subsequent inquiries about the status of this investigation have yielded unsatisfactory results. The bank has informed me that they can not locate a record of my earlier cancellation, despite my clear recollection and previous correspondence. Ally Bank was using the cancellations that were processed on or after XX/XX/XXXX ( those that were done after the fact to correct the problem that had already occurred ) as an excuse not to investigate the issue. The text of the original report that Ally Banks representative had filed as case number XXXX also can not be located by the banks representatives. The bank had claimed that no cancellations of the recurring transfer had occurred ( according to the records that they have ) prior to XX/XX/XXXX.
It is important to note that if the recurring transfer had not been canceled prior to XX/XX/XXXX, it would have been expected to be processed on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. The fact that no such transfers occurred during these periods strongly suggests that a cancellation was indeed in place. The attached correspondence with Ally Bank shows that they are aware that a cancellation of the recurring transfer originally scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX has happened on XX/XX/XXXX, however, to my additional questions whether, according to the Ally Banks records, that cancellation was for the entire recurring sequence of transfers or not Ally Bank has not responded.

In one of the conversations I had with Ally Bank on XX/XX/XXXX I asked Ally Banks representatives to check the history of the email communications that Ally Bank had with me with regards to my account activities. Such correspondence history should have included all confirmations of my XXXX transfers cancellation requests and would help Ally Bank to identify using their internal data on what dates my recurring transfer sequence cancellation requests had occurred. Ally Banks representative informed that they do not have access to the history of the email communications prior to XX/XX/XXXX. During that conversation I had made a request for reasonable accommodations under the Americans with Disabilities Act to Ally Bank to restore my email communications with Ally Bank since XX/XX/XXXX ( request reference number XXXX ). In the attached document I am enclosing Ally Banks Executive Customer Relations representatives response to my request claiming that Ally Bank can not accommodate it and not providing any explanation of whether granting me the requested reasonable accommodation would constitute an undue burden on the bank or would require the bank to fundamentally alter the nature of the service it provides to the public.

I believe that the described incident of Ally Banks attempting to perform a transfer that was canceled by the customer months prior and the banks inability to properly investigate the cause of that is indicative of a serious flaw in Ally Bank 's or its vendor 's system, which is allowing unauthorized transfer attempts to occur. Furthermore, the bank 's failure to investigate this issue thoroughly and its apparent deletion of relevant requests history demonstrate the Banks lack of due diligence.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Complaint Submission : Issue with Truist Bank Regarding {$500.00} Payment and Lack of Communication To : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Regarding : Truist Bank **Date : XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX  Subject : Truist Banks Mishandling of a {$500.00} Payment to XXXX  XXXX, Resulting in Financial Harm Due to Lack of Communication and Error Details : I am filing a formal complaint against Truist Bank due to their mishandling of a {$500.00} payment I attempted to make to my XXXX XXXXXXXX account, which resulted in financial harm and potential negative impact on my creditworthiness. Below is a detailed account of the events and issues.

1. Timeline of Events : - On XXXX XXXX XXXX I attempted to make a {$500.00} payment from my Truist checking account to my XXXX XXXX credit card account.
- Upon submitting the payment, I observed that the {$500.00} was deducted from my available balance. My account balance showed that I had approximately {$0.00} left, indicating that the payment was processed.
- I was under the impression that the payment had been successfully initiated and was on track to be credited to my XXXX XXXX account.

2. Issue with Transaction Posting : - A few days later, I checked my Truist account and noticed that the {$500.00} payment was no longer reflected in my transaction history. It had completely disappeared from my recent transactions list, and the balance no longer showed that the {$500.00} had been deducted.
- This disappearance led me to question what had happened to the payment, as I never received any notification from Truist that the payment was declined, reversed, or had bounced. I assumed that since the money had left my account and I had the funds available at the time, the payment would have gone through.

3. Truist 's Explanation and Lack of Communication : - After contacting Truist Bank to inquire about the missing {$500.00} payment, I was told by a representative that the payment had not actually left my account and that it had been declined due to insufficient funds. According to the representative, the bank used its discretion to avoid an overdraft situation because my account was short by {$0.00} at the time of the payment processing.
- The representative further explained that the {$500.00} did not go through because there was a separate pending transaction from an earlier purchase, which included a tip. However, this explanation does not align with my understanding or observations. When I initially made the {$500.00} payment, the funds were in my account, and the {$500.00} had already been deducted from my balance. I was not informed at any point that the funds were insufficient or that the payment was being declined due to this separate transaction.

4. Lack of Notification and Financial Harm : - At no point did Truist notify me that the {$500.00} payment had been declined or that my account had insufficient funds. This lack of communication caused significant confusion and led me to believe that the payment had been successfully processed.
- I was not given any opportunity to address the alleged shortfall of {$0.00} because Truist failed to inform me of the issue. As a result, the payment to XXXX XXXX did not go through, and I was left with the false belief that my XXXX XXXX account had been credited with the {$500.00}.
- Truist also failed to notify XXXX XXXX that the payment had bounced or was declined. This lack of communication between the banks has resulted in further complications for me, as my XXXX XXXX account was never credited, and I am now at risk of late payment fees and damage to my credit score due to a payment that I attempted to make in good faith.

5. Discrepancy Between Account Balance and Payment : - I have consistently informed Truist representatives that I saw the {$500.00} leave my account, and my balance reflected this deduction. Truists claim that the money never left the account does not match what I observed. There is clearly a discrepancy between what was shown in my account and what Truist is now claiming.
- If Truist had properly informed me that there was a problem with the payment, I could have resolved the issue immediately by transferring additional funds to cover the {$0.00} shortfall. Instead, their failure to notify me has caused financial hardship, stress, and confusion.

6. Financial and Credit Impact : - The failure to process the {$500.00} payment has directly impacted my ability to pay my XXXX XXXX bill, potentially leading to late fees, penalties, and damage to my credit score. This could have been entirely avoided if Truist had communicated with me promptly and accurately.
- Additionally, I am concerned that this error may affect my ability to access credit in the future due to potential negative marks on my credit report. This situation has caused me financial harm through no fault of my own.

End Conclusion : Truist Banks failure to notify me of the declined payment, coupled with the disappearance of the {$500.00} transaction from my account history, has caused significant financial harm and stress. I acted in good faith, believing that I had successfully made the payment, and Truists error has led to complications that are not my fault. I request that the CFPB investigate this issue and ensure that Truist takes appropriate action to resolve it.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I appreciate your assistance in resolving this issue.
XXXX XXXX Account number : XXXX

XXXX XXXX XXXX Ally Bank is negligent of not investigating previously canceled recurring XXXX transfers that reappear several months after they were canceled and result in unauthorized attempts to make withdrawals from customers accounts. In addition, Ally Banks record keeping practices regarding customers requests to schedule, modify, and cancel recurring XXXX transfers are weak as they allow deletion of the information.

Dear CFPB, I am writing to express my deep concern regarding an attempt of Ally Bank to transfer funds in amount of {$2700.00} from my checking account on XX/XX/XXXX, which Ally Bank claims were part of a recurring monthly XXXX transfer. This incident is troubling given the fact that I had explicitly canceled the entire recurring transfer series in question on XX/XX/XXXX.

On XX/XX/XXXX I had scheduled a recurring monthly XXXX transfer in the amount of {$2700.00} from my checking account ending in XXXX to one of my XXXX recipients XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) at XXXX with the initial transfer anticipated to occur on XX/XX/XXXX and then on the XXXX of every subsequent month ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, etc ). However, subsequently I canceled the entire series of the recurring transfers on XX/XX/XXXX. In addition, in XX/XX/XXXX, I canceled absolutely all remaining ( I had at least three other recurring XXXX transfers with Ally Bank at that time ) recurring transfers associated with my account. At the end of XX/XX/XXXX I also verified through my online banking that no outstanding future scheduled XXXX transfers were showing up on my account.

Despite these clear cancellations, there was an unexpected attempt to make a XXXX transfer from my account on XX/XX/XXXX for the amount matching the previously canceled recurring transfer. When I contacted Ally Bank and learned that my account was blocked due to not enough funds to cover the transfer I informed Ally Bank that the transfer attempt was clearly a banks error since I had canceled all recurring transfers months prior. The Ally Banks representative I was conversing with acknowledged the cancellation I had made in XXXX and indicated that they would file a report ( case number XXXX ) so that Ally Bank would investigate the matter.

However, my subsequent inquiries about the status of this investigation have yielded unsatisfactory results. The bank has informed me that they can not locate a record of my earlier cancellation, despite my clear recollection and previous correspondence. Ally Bank was using the cancellations that were processed on or after XX/XX/XXXX ( those that were done after the fact to correct the problem that had already occurred ) as an excuse not to investigate the issue. The text of the original report that Ally Banks representative had filed as case number XXXX also can not be located by the banks representatives. The bank had claimed that no cancellations of the recurring transfer had occurred ( according to the records that they have ) prior to XX/XX/XXXX.
It is important to note that if the recurring transfer had not been canceled prior to XX/XX/XXXX, it would have been expected to be processed on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. The fact that no such transfers occurred during these periods strongly suggests that a cancellation was indeed in place. The attached correspondence with Ally Bank shows that they are aware that a cancellation of the recurring transfer originally scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX has happened on XX/XX/XXXX, however, to my additional questions whether, according to the Ally Banks records, that cancellation was for the entire recurring sequence of transfers or not Ally Bank has not responded.

In one of the conversations I had with Ally Bank on XX/XX/XXXX I asked Ally Banks representatives to check the history of the email communications that Ally Bank had with me with regards to my account activities. Such correspondence history should have included all confirmations of my XXXX transfers cancellation requests and would help Ally Bank to identify using their internal data on what dates my recurring transfer sequence cancellation requests had occurred. Ally Banks representative informed that they do not have access to the history of the email communications prior to XX/XX/XXXX. During that conversation I had made a request for reasonable accommodations under the Americans with Disabilities Act to Ally Bank to restore my email communications with Ally Bank since XX/XX/XXXX ( request reference number XXXX ). In the attached document I am enclosing Ally Banks Executive Customer Relations representatives response to my request claiming that Ally Bank can not accommodate it and not providing any explanation of whether granting me the requested reasonable accommodation would constitute an undue burden on the bank or would require the bank to fundamentally alter the nature of the service it provides to the public.

I believe that the described incident of Ally Banks attempting to perform a transfer that was canceled by the customer months prior and the banks inability to properly investigate the cause of that is indicative of a serious flaw in Ally Bank 's or its vendor 's system, which is allowing unauthorized transfer attempts to occur. Furthermore, the bank 's failure to investigate this issue thoroughly and its apparent deletion of relevant requests history demonstrate the Banks lack of due diligence.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

I am available to provide any additional information or clarification as needed. Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated. I look forward to your response and the resolution of this serious issue.

narrative:
BACKGROUND : Our mortgage had been in place for seven years. We have always maintained full property insurance, as required. Our insurer and agent has always sent the servicer proper advise of our continuous coverage. Meanwhile, the servicer has claimed repeatedly that our insurance was expired or non-existent. The most recent occurrance of this behavior is exhibited on FORM LETTER # XXXX attached dated XX/XX/24.

After receiving letter # XXXX, we first followed up to the servicer by telephone. In that phone call, the XXXX told us verbally what insurance information they had " on file '' for us. The information they conveyed was completely erroneous ( wrong insurer name and wrong expiration date )! We became very concerned about fraud and identity theft, because it appeared the XXXX had mixed up our records with those from a different customer. Where else could the false insurer and expiration data come from?

For this latest false claim by the Servicer ( letter # XXXX ), we sent the Servicer letter # XXXX dated XX/XX/24 attached. Considering the past and current inaccurate Servicer recordkeeping, our letter reasonably requested that the servicer provide us with a summary of past entries, changes and data sources for the insurance information they had entered on our account records. Only by seeing this infromation can we compare it to insurance information sent to the servicer by agents and insurers. Only by doing this can we get evidence of the accuracy or ineptitude of the servicer 's recordkeeping.

Subsequently, we received the servicer 's FORM LETTER # XXXX dated XX/XX/24, indicating they now purportedly had accurate insurance information even though ( a ) they had accurate information it all along until they mixed up our records with those of other customers and ( b ) THEY DID NOT DISCLOSE WHAT INFORMATION THEY NOW HAVE so we have no way of knowing just WHAT their records show now.

The servicing provided on this mortgage loan - as it relates to insurance monitoring- is sloppy at best. At worst, it puts customer privacy at risk because customer data is not accurately updated and maintained.
