Subject : Urgent : Unjust and predatory practices by Village Capital Dear XXXX, I am a 100 % XXXX veteran and I am reaching out to you with deep concern and frustration regarding my ongoing experience with Village Capital, my current mortgage holder ; My situation has escalated over this past year, and I believe it is imperative that you understand the gravity of what I've been subjected to : In XXXX of XXXX, I began the process of refinancing my home. Initially, I was advised to withhold my XXXX payment, as we were assured that closing would occur within days. Unfortunately, the loan fell through, and I found myself in a precarious position. I was then introduced to XXXX, who worked tirelessly to secure a new loan for me. But, despite her efforts and multiple communications with my creditors, the loan never materialized due to unforeseen obstacles.

During this time, I reached out to Village Capital 's loss mitigation department to arrange bringing my account current. I followed through on the agreed-upon terms, yet my XX/XX/XXXX payment was inexplicably placed in " suspense '' and not honored until after 30 days, putting me in arrears through no fault of my own.

Now, a year later, in an attempt to refinance again, I was denied due to a XXXX late payment that was in reality not late at all ; This situation has continued to spiral out of control. In XX/XX/XXXX, following an audit by Village Capital, my monthly payment suddenly jumped from {$1400.00} to nearly {$1700.00}. I paid the regular amount and separately covered the {$270.00} difference. By XXXX my payment had inexplicably risen to {$1800.00}, despite no legitimate in my insurance or property taxes. In fact, both had in fact decreased, which I confirmed directly with my insurance provider and the county assessor 's office.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a call from Village Capital 's collection department demanding {$4000.00}. The following day, Village Capital called offering an in-house refinance, suggesting they would drop the discrepancy - an offer that only added to my confusion and distress. Now, despite my mortgage documents clearly stating a regular, fixed, payment of {$1400.00}, I am being asked to pay {$1800.00} plus an additional {$490.00}, with no clear or honest explanation.

It seems clear to me that Village Capital is engaging in predatory practices, exploiting the most vulnerable, including veterans like myself. My sole source of income is my VA benefits plus XXXX Social Security, which Village Capital already takes 25 % of. The stress, heartache, and sleepless nights caused by Village Capital 's actions have become overwhelming. I believe Village Capital is attempting to force me out of my home by any means necessary, and this is something I can not, and will not, stand for.

As a veteran who has served this country, I refuse to be bullied, abused or taken advantage of. This is not just about me, this is about protecting all veterans and homeowners who may be facing similar harassment. I firmly believe that Village Capital should be removed from XXXX XXXXXXXX, and I will be pursuing all avenues to ensure that justice is served.

I have compiled extensive documentation, including payment history, letters from my insurance and tax offices and all relevant correspondence with XXXX XXXX. I am prepared to share this evidence with the VA and any other relevant authorities.

This situation is intolerable, and I am seeking a resolution that not only addresses my case but also sends a clear message to XXXX XXXX and any other lenders that such treatment will not be tolerated. I urge you to take immediate action and to rectify this situation and protect those who have served our country eith honor.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response.

Sincerely

narrative:

Credit Score > XXXX 2. Loan to value less than 10 % ( Citibank appraisal of {$1.00} XXXX ).
3. No existing mortgage debt ( subject property is free and clear ).
4. Own a second home free and clear ( value of {$670.00} thousand ).
5. Income is derived from two pensions, Social Security, part-time work, and income/dividends from investment accounts.

My credit history is unblemished, I good liquidity, low debt and a good net worth.

A counteroffer was made at {$38000.00}, apparently based on the fact that my debt-to-income ratio was too high. I contacted the mortgage representative and indicated that I had {$220.00} XXXX in liquid assets and {$1.00} XXXX in an IRA with XXXX XXXX, which I felt had not been considered in their analysis of the loan request. Furthermore, I indicated that I would have to take Required Minimum Distributions from the IRA beginning in XXXX which would be approximately {$75.00} to {$80.00} XXXX annually. I also stated that funds in the IRA could be withdrawn at any time without penalty to support payment of any debt.

On XX/XX/XXXX, the HELOC request was declined for : 1 ) Insufficient income for the total obligation and 2 ) Insufficient Citi banking relationship for the loan request.

I believe that Citibank 's underwriting practices are discriminatory in that other sources of income such as withdrawals from IRA accounts whether discretionary or required are not taken into consideration, and that accommodation is not made for such potential source of income.

Subsequent to the decline of the HELOC application, Citibank has sent me two mortgage loan offers ( up to {$2000.00} off on closing costs ). This seems incongruent and is insulting.

sub_product: Home equity loan or line of credit (HELOC)
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during the application process
narrative: I applied for a home equity loan from Nationstar Mortgage in XX/XX/year>. The process was protracted and there were multiple increases in the quoted rate. I ultimately chose not to move forward with the loan application. The issue is that in XX/XX/year>, before I had agreed to the loan or signed anything other than my disclosure acknowledgments, Nationstar contacted my home insurer, XXXX, represented itself as the holder of a second, perfected mortgage note on my home, and inserted itself as the second mortgagee on my home insurance policy without my knowledge or permission. It did not call XXXX back in XXXX, after we ended the loan application process, to remove itself. It appears that Nationstar was trying to entitle itself to an insurance payout it did not deserve in the event of a catastrophic event.

My monthly home mortgage rate has been ~ {$6200.00} since we purchased our home in XX/XX/year>. Last year, our bike was stolen from the front of our house and we had a minor theft reported while travelling. Both were reported to home insurance. Because of that, my home insurance ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) declined to renew our coverage last XXXX. My mortgage servicer, US Bank, claims they tried to inform me of this change via mail. When told that I never received any notification, and upon being asked to show proof they tried to communicate with me via email or phone ( my preferred method of communication and the way they communicate with me for all other reasons ), neither they nor XXXX were able to show me any proof.

As a result of my home insurance being cancelled, US Bank then elected to sign me up for their own home
insurance plan, which was roughly 4.5x the cost of my previous plan ( {$800.00} per month, instead of {$160.00} per month ) per. BUT, they did not actually start charging me this on a monthly basis until XXXX, when my escrow was fully depleted and they conducted a new escrow analysis. XXXX Bank did not provide explanation for why they waited this long to conduct a new escrow analysis, which does not make sense since they knew my escrow would quickly be exhausted by the new plan.

In XXXX, my monthly mortgage payment suddenly jumped from ~ {$6200.00} to ~ {$7800.00}. As soon as I discovered why, I changed my insurance that day to another XXXX plan for {$220.00} a month, nearly {$600.00} cheaper than the plan US Bank forced me to sign up for. And because I didn't find out about it until XXXX, I accrued nearly XXXX XXXX in extra expenses, that US Bank is now charging me for to catch up to my old account.

US Bank acknowledges I never consented to the higher rate, and would not provide any proof it tried to contact me via email, despite acknowledging that is how I elected to receive notification. US Bank also refused to provide any reimbursement, despite no claims being filed
on my home during this period. XXXX also did not provide me any explanation as to why they did not communicate with me via email, despite using email for all other communication up to that point ( including sharing my policy with me when I first signed up ).

I find this practice highly misleading and unfair - I never knew about my insurance cancellation, no one tried to contact me via email or phone, and they signed me up for a much more expensive plan without my consent th
at cost me nearly XXXX XXXX

As per the forbearance laws that were set in place from XXXX, there were to be multiple ways in order to repay the forbearance amount.

You have denied our right to use any of the other options that were meant by law to be part of the forbearance terms and conditions which should have included the following : If you have a XXXX XXXX or XXXX XXXXbacked loan, you have five post-forbearance options : Reinstatement : Youll repay all the payments you missed right away after your forbearance.

Repayment plan : Youll establish a repayment plan and pay off a portion of your missed payments each month until the entire missed amount is accounted for.

Deferral : Youll defer the missed payments until the maturity date on your loan when they become due as a non-interest-bearing balance.

Flex Modification program : Your servicer will permanently extend your loans term or lower your interest rate to lower your monthly payment.

Refinance : Youll obtain a new loan with a new interest rate and term. To qualify, youll need to be officially off the forbearance plan, have made at least three consecutive monthly payments and meet the lenders eligibility requirements.

And now unfortunately we are not eligible for refinance, because our account was illegally locked immediately
when the forbearance plan ended with XXXX XXXX and then remained locked by SPS when it was sold to them in XX/XX/XXXX. In our copy of the official " NOTE '' that was sent to us by SPS, it states that " the borrower has the right to make payments at any time ''.

issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
sub_issue: Confusing or misleading advertising or marketing
narrative:
On XX/XX/2024, I received a loan estimate from Better.com for a mortgage on my property. Before receiving this estimate, I was repeatedly assured by the company 's representative, XXXX, that all costs except for Prepaids ( F ) and Initial Escrow Payment ( G ) would be covered by lender credits. Based on this information, I decided to lock in the mortgage rate with them.

However, upon reviewing the loan estimate today, I found that additional costs were included that were supposed to be covered by the lender credits, contrary to what was promised. This has caused unexpected financial concerns, as I was explicitly told that these costs would not apply.

Immediately after reviewing the estimate, I made multiple attempts to contact XXXX via phone and text messages throughout the day to clarify this discrepancy, but I have received no response. I also reached out to Better.com to escalate the issue, but I have not yet received any communication or resolution from the company.

Due to this misleading information and lack of response, I feel I have been misled about the true costs associated with this mortgage. I have requested that Better.com either adjust the loan terms to reflect the promised lender credits or cancel the loan process altogether with a full refund of my {$550.00} fee. Additionally, I seek compensation for the hard credit inquiry on my account and the time wasted due to this issue.

As of now, Better.com has not taken any steps to address or resolve my concerns.

I was contacted by Chase Bank by letter that they had determined that my condominium address is in a flood zone, and therefore, I must buy flood insurance to protect their interest in the condo unit. Oddly, they also wrote that FEMA determined my home was in a flood zone.

If I did not purchase flood insurance for them, they would do so for me and pass the costs on to me by raising my mortgage. They then wrote that if I believe I'm not in a flood zone, to contact FEMA and to request an amendment or revision to the flood map ( XXXX or XXXX ) and that I would have to pay for any costs associated with getting them to revise the map and send proof. I went to the FEMA website, entered my address and it showed that I was NOT in a flood zone as of XX/XX/year>. So I'm not sure what they are supposed to revise or amend.

I called Chase to get more information and the person I spoke to reiterated that FEMA made this determination. I point her to the FEMA website and she said she was not sure how to read the map. She asked me to contact FEMA and to go through the XXXX process.

I called FEMA and the person I spoke to said ( 1 ) that FEMA does not communicate with banks about flood map updates, and ( 2 ) Chase bank has a third party they contract with that makes the " Standard Flood Hazard Determinations '' ... or something like that. This XXXX representative then went to their maps and confirmed that my home is not in a flood zone. I asked XXXX what to do and they recommended doing both of the following actions : ( 1 ) Go through the XXXX process but that it takes 60 days ; ( 2 ) File a complaint with the regulatory body over the organization that Chase contracts with to make the determinations, and to contact the state attorney general. FEMA gave me a number to call.

I called that number, went through a long menu of option, spoke to someone who then told me that it does sound like Chase is engaged in forced-insurance. She then said the best entity to help me through this is the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau.

I decided to contact Chase one more time. I explained the situation, and relayed to them what FEMA said and that FEMA denies communicating with them directly ( contradicting what the previous Chase representative said ). This new Chase rep admits that it was another entity that makes that determination, and reiterated the only remedy available to me is to fill out the XXXX forms at my expense. She also reiterated that if I don't buy a flood insurance policy for Chase, they would do it for me, and pass the costs to me, even though it'll take 2 months before FEMA can send proof that I'm not in a flood zone. She mentioned something about offering a refund if they were wrong, but that seems to contradict aspects of what is written in the letter, particularly where there's a gap in coverage.

In short, as best as I understand it, Chase contracts with some 3rd Party to make determinations about whether a property is in a flood zone, then Chase claims that it was FEMA who made that determination, then demands homeowners to buy flood insurance to protect Chase 's interest in the property. If the homeowner doesn't comply, Chase will buy what they openly admit may not be the most affordable insurance. To comply with their demand, they give homeowners LESS than the 60 days it takes to go through the XXXX process ( which you may have to pay for ) to prove you are not in the flood zone, even though ANYONE can simply go to the FEMA website, enter their address, and see that they are not in a flood zone.

I did a quick XXXX search and it seems Chase has a history of doing this.

This is not at all fair.

Third time requesting.

I have no 30 day lates in the past 12 months and no 60 day lates in the last 24 months.

I have not been 30 days late in the past 12 months. I was not considered " late '' when refinancing via the Federal Govt. 2 years ago.

Please supply this information - 3rd time requesting.

As a local XXXX, I'm well aware of home values in my region and the reported value did not align with my professional opinion. sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue with the loan closing
narrative:
XX/XX/year> XXXX, and XXXX of Wells Fargo, Thank you for allowing me the opportunity to present my case today. I stand before you to address a series of actions and decisions by Wells Fargo that I believe have been grossly mishandled, resulting in an unjust denial of my claim for wrongful Foreclosure as my loan modification had been approved and finalized however, they proceeded to foreclose anyway, and for obstruction of my right to a fair mediation process.
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Wells Fargo with a check for {$1000.00} stating there had been an error on my loan modification that had been approved and finalized. I proceeded to verify the check was valid and contacted the number that was on the letter. My information was put in the system and was told I would be contacted by a case manager.
On XX/XX/year>, I spoke with XXXX XXXX, who called and assured me that my case would be reviewed and that I would receive a response by XX/XX/year>. Despite this commitment, I never received a follow-up call. Instead, I received a letter with a Mediation claim form, which I completed and submitted via fax on XX/XX/year>, that was 14 pages of supporting documentation, including mortgage and checking account information pertinent to my dispute, in addition to this it was also mailed I the posted paid envelope provided by Wells Fargo.
On XX/XX/year>, I was contacted by XXXX XXXX, who informed me that she had taken over my case from XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX proceeded to hastily inform me that Wells Fargo had decided to deny my claim. When I inquired about the appeal process, she curtly informed me that no such process existed and that I would receive a letter confirming the denial. XXXX XXXX did not offer any further explanation, nor did she inquire if I had any questions, which left me with significant concerns about the transparency and fairness of this process.
Later that day, after reflecting on the conversation, I called back to verify the validity of the call and to speak directly with XXXX XXXX. I was informed by another Wells Fargo representative that XXXX XXXX was not handling my case, and that XXXX XXXX was indeed the one in charge. Upon being transferred back to XXXX XXXX, I requested to speak with either XXXX XXXX or the Program Administrator. XXXX XXXX implied that she was working within the Highest Executive Office and could not provide any further details, including the timeframe for when I would be contacted about the next steps in the mediation process.
I pressed for more information about the mediation process, but XXXX XXXX was unable to provide a clear explanation or a confirmation number for our call, which further eroded my trust in the process. Despite having submitted the Mediation claim form on XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX appeared unaware of this, and stated she does not know what XXXX XXXX and I had discussed, leading me to believe that there is a deliberate effort to prevent my case from being fairly heard in mediation.
Given that the deceptive practices are continuing with Wells Fargo, throughout this process, I am now requesting that the punitive damages be increased to four times the original amount requested. This increase reflects the severity of the situation and the need for accountability in how Wells Fargo has handled my case and future cases, wanting to go through meditation.
On XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX I called again at XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX and spoke with XXXX XXXX again to verify if my mediation form had been received and processed. I also asked to speak with XXXX XXXX and was told she know longer works in the department for Wells Fargo. XXXX stated that she did not know anything about meditation, and her boss had instructed her to call me and give me the results of XXXX XXXX findings and could not verify if my mediation form had been processed or received. XXXX XXXX also stated that she had to open a new case for me, and she knew nothing about the mediation process. I pressed her on why a new case has to be open. She stated that when XXXX XXXX finished her findings the case was closed. Why would the case be closed when you have not come to a resolve with the customer or to see if they wanted to go to mediation? She stated that my case had to be reopened and I will be hearing from someone from Wells Fargo within 24 to 48 hours. She still could not verify if my meditation form had been reached by the Mediation Program Administrator.
Furthermore, I formally request that all calls related to this matter be pulled and reviewed, along with the notes on this case, as evidence of my conversations throughout this process. These recordings will clearly demonstrate the mishandling and lack of transparency that I have experienced.
I respectfully ask that this mediation of my mortgage case proceed with full transparency and that my concerns regarding the handling of my case be addressed. I seek a fair resolution and an opportunity to present the facts of my case in a manner that allows for a just outcome.

1. Did not offer Due Process of Mediation and stated there is no appeals process available.

2. Implied that she was in the Program Administrator Office ; by stating she was with the Highest Executive Office at Wells Fargo.

3. Did not offer any direction or timing of the next steps in the mediation process.

4. Was not able to give me an appointment, date or time for mediation 5. Had to ask what the timeframe for medication and getting an appointment she then started with the next 24 to 48 hours.

sub_issue: Closing disclosure or other related disclosures
narrative:
I requested a refund of the appraisal fee that I was charged during a recent mortgage application process with Residential Mortgage Services. I believe the fee was unjustly charged due to the mishandling of my loan application and the failure to provide the required documentation and information, as outlined below. XXXX XXXX was my lender and XXXX XXXX is the manager for the branch. Ive been upfront from the beginning on my employment contracts, and stating that Im an employee contracted to work during the school year. I had a pre approval already from XXXX XXXX  but went with residential mortgage because of a grant program offered. After residential mortgage failed, to finance me I now own the home through a XXXX XXXX loan. Isnt that ironic.
Background : The appraisal fee of {$700.00} was requested and paid on XX/XX/2024, under the assumption that all preliminary underwriting checks had been completed and that the loan was likely to proceed. However, it was not until XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX days after the appraisal fee was paidthat Residential Mortgage asked for my school contracts and discovered that my employment is for 9 months out of the year, which ultimately led to the loan being denied.

Residential Mortgage failed to conduct proper due diligence before requesting the appraisal fee. Information regarding my employment contract should have been reviewed prior to ordering the appraisal. If this critical information had been considered earlier, it would have been clear that the loan could not be approved under their criteria, and the appraisal fee would not have been necessary.
Issues : XXXX. Mishandling of Loan Application : Despite my upfront disclosure regarding my employment status, Residential Mortgage Services proceeded with ordering the appraisal immediately after receiving my school contracts, which confirmed my nine-month work schedule. Instead of pausing the appraisal order to reassess the situation, Residential Mortgage Services moved forward, leading to a situation where I was later informed that my loan could not be approved.
XXXX. Lack of Formal Denial : The only communication I received from XXXX XXXX was an email stating, Per underwriting, we arent going to be able to approve this loan. I never received an official denial letter, which I believe is required by law under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( ECOA ). This act requires lenders to provide a list of the main reasons for denying a loan or inform the applicant of their right to request this information. When I asked for a denial letter, I was not provided with an official one.
XXXX. Misleading Guidance : After receiving the vague denial statement from XXXX XXXX, I was advised to pay down debt and try again next year. Had I followed this guidance, I would have lost the opportunity to purchase the home I now own, thanks to securing financing with another lender. This further demonstrates the flawed handling of my application by Residential Mortgage Services.
Request : Given these circumstances, I request a full refund of the appraisal fee. I believe it is only fair, considering the appraisal was ordered prematurely and without proper consideration of the underwriting issues that were present at the time.

Speechless.

XXXX, the representative told me that my request for forbearance had been denied. I was confused because I never requested a forbearance, I thought that it was something that a servicing company needs to suggest, not from me. When I inquired about the reasons of the denial, I was told that one of the criteria for forbearance is being three to four months late on payments, which was my situation. The other criteria was a cash down payment. However, I was never informed of the amount required for this payment. Had I been made aware, I would have found the necessary funds to avoid the current situation. It is deeply troubling that I was not offered forbearance or informed of the necessary steps to qualify for it, especially considering that most of the servicing companies exhaust all possible options to avoid foreclosure. In my case, this was not done.

DEATH IN FAMILY In XX/XX/XXXX, my mother passed away in XXXX. I had to travel to XXXX to attend her funeral at the end of XXXX, which placed a significant financial burden on me. Additionally, the official XXXX  took place in XX/XX/XXXX. Unfortunately, due to my financial difficulties, I was unable to attend this important family event, which has been emotionally devastating.

HEALTH ISSUES Compounding my situation, I am also dealing with serious health issues. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX This condition has left me feeling extremely uncomfortable and has severely impacted my ability to work as a XXXX XXXX XXXX. As you can imagine, my self-esteem and professional capacity have been greatly affected, leading to a further decline in my income. The cost to repair is {$30000.00} to {$40000.00} - I can not afford this amount for the moment.

FLOOD & FEMA In XX/XX/XXXX, my property was flooded in the kitchen due to extreme rain in XXXX XXXX. I had to deal with FEMA, and it took over a year to finally resolve this situation with them. Unfortunately, the financial assistance from FEMA was limited and did not cover the full extent of the damage. As a result, I had to use my own money and borrow from friends to repair the damage and make my home livable, especially to prevent mold from developing in the kitchen.

XXXX INCREASE / SPECIAL ASSESSMENTS XXXX XXXX monthly increase The most significant factors contributing to my financial hardship, however, have been the dramatic increases in my housing costs. Since XX/XX/XXXX, my condo fees increased from {$560.00} to {$680.00} per month. I have also been required to pay a special assessment of {$480.00} per month. A second special assessment for the XXXX of {$500.00}. This means my total monthly XXXX fees, including the special assessments, have skyrocketed from {$560.00} to {$1200.00} per month.

MORTGAGE INCREASE XXXX XXXX  monthly increase In addition to these challenges, my mortgage payment, which was {$670.00} per month, increased to {$1200.00} per month. This increase occurred because PHH Mortgage Services paid my property taxes for last year, that I need to repay and PHH Mortgage Services requested to cumulate for the projected taxes for next year. As a result, my mortgage payment rose by {$550.00} per month.

Altogether, these increases in my mortgage payment, condo fees, and special assessments have added {$1200.00} to my monthly expenses, making it nearly impossible for me to keep up with my financial obligations.

FILLING TO COURT FOR FORECLOSURE 5 MONTHS AFTER BEING LATE FOR THE FIRST TIME On XX/XX/XXXX, the last day that I was under review of the Modification Offer, PHH Mortgage Services filled in Court a lawsuit against me for non payment of XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, and request a foreclosure because I did not respond to the offer at that time.

Moreover, it is extremely challenging for me to defend myself in this foreclosure process, as I simply do not have the funds to hire a lawyer. PHH Mortgage Services is fully aware of my financial hardship, yet by proceeding with foreclosure, it is effectively discriminating against me, knowing that I can not afford adequate legal representation. This further emphasizes the unfairness of the situation, where a forbearance could have been a reasonable and humane solution.

EXPLANATION FOR NON-RESPONSE TO LOAN MODIFICATION OFFER XXXX XXXX Here is the reason for my non-response to the loan modification offer provided by PHH Mortgage Services. The circumstances surrounding my decision were directly influenced by advice I received from XXXX XXXX, the owner of my mortgage, specifically from a caseworker named XXXX.

Upon receiving the letter dated XX/XX/XXXX of a Loan Modification Offer, I was surprised, as I had been expecting a forbearance option to be presented instead. Concerned about this discrepancy, I contacted XXXX XXXX to clarify the situation. During my conversation with XXXX, the caseworker that I was transferred to, I explained my concerns, particularly the fact that the first loan modification offered only reduced my payments by XXXX XXXXa reduction that would not significantly alleviate my financial burden.

XXXX assured me that she would request a second loan modification from PHH Mortgage Services, as she understood that the initial offer was insufficient. She explicitly instructed me not to respond to the first loan modification offer by the XX/XX/XXXX deadline. She explained that submitting a response to the first offer could create confusion in the system and increase the likelihood of errors. She also advised me not to contact customer service regarding this matter, as she believed the first loan modification would be automatically canceled once the request for the second modification was submitted.

Following XXXXXXXX XXXX guidance, I did not respond to the initial loan modification offer. Unfortunately, despite my compliance with her instructions, the day that marked the final deadline to accept the modification also became the day my property was filed for foreclosure in court.

When I raised this issue with XXXXXXXX XXXX supervisor, XXXX XXXX, I received no assistance, nor did she acknowledge the error.

I firmly believe that the guidance provided by XXXX XXXX XXXX representative, XXXX, is the sole reason for my non-response, and as such, I hold XXXX XXXX and PHH Mortgage Services responsible for the resulting foreclosure action and destroyed my creditworthiness.

To ensure that all parties are aware of this situation and to seek a fair resolution, I am copying this letter to the Office of the Attorney General of Florida, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), the Florida Department of Financial Services, XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX and the Federal Housing Finance Agency ( FHFA ). I hope that by bringing this matter to their attention, PHH Mortgage Services will take the appropriate steps to rectify this situation and provide the necessary assistance.

I made XXXX complaints to the bank without response.

Today I called the bank again and they told me that I had to make arrangements to contact the lawyer who did the closing in 2004 to make a new deed and present it, that the arrangement wasn't with them.

narrative:
Retaliation for initial complaint Company : Loan Depot This complaint is about a va loan where I was reported incorrectly to the credit bureaus since I was in a forbearance and current at the time the forbearance was approved, a data breech, the timing of the transfer to XXXX and unnecessary delays caused by loan depot.
Loan Depot reported me as seriously delinquent on XX/XX/XXXX when I had a forbearance and was actively working with Loan Depot. I called them multiple times and was waiting for them to review an application to assume my loan. This took loan depot 4 months and then XXXX another XXXX months. An outrageously long time for an application to be reviewed.
Why I needed a forbearance I had to stop working in XX/XX/XXXX due to increasing XXXX. This XXXX increased in XX/XX/XXXX and I had to take a few months off work and it was related to the covid pandemic and isolation. I took an early retirement for my health and applied for a forbearance on XX/XX/XXXX ( during the 120 days after the XXXX XXXX order was lifted ( so the XXXX XXXX  applies ). On XX/XX/XXXX, the loss mitigation application was approved. My loan was current at the time of the loss mitigation request and I was never behind in payments and I had a plan to finish the remodel and sell the home.
The viable plan to have the buyers assume the loan On XX/XX/XXXX, the buyers submitted their loan assumption application. The plan was for them to pay the forbearance and take over the loan and pay all fees. My rate was XXXX XXXX until year XXXX then XXXX XXXX increase annually to XXXX of XXXX XXXX
Loan servicer delays The loan servicer was unable to review the application until late XXXX ( almost 3 months of no review ). How do I know this? XXXX XXXX ( spelling may be incorrect ), who was the assumption servicer contact and only person able to review the assumption, told me that she could not get to the application until late XXXX due to multiple applications prior to mine and she was having to train staff in the department.
I called multiple times. I requested to extend my forbearance since the application process was being delayed by loan depot. I told the servicer that every month the forbearance increases by {$4000.00}. Loan depot stated the application was being worked on since XXXX. However, the assumptions representative XXXX XXXX, who was the only XXXX assigned to this application told me the application was not being worked on and was not put in the computer yet in XXXX.
The only review that was done in XXXX was by underwriting who told the servicer that the assumption could not be approved since there was money owed and I was late with my payments. In fact, XXXX XXXX did not call me with that information. Instead, she called the buyers and told them. This was my first realization of the misaligned goals with Loan Depot.
I called XXXX XXXX and we argued about the ability to assume the loan. I had asked before I went this route and was told it was assumable and loan depot sent the application. By XXXX, my home was in contract and my realtor stopped showing the house. My VA representative, XXXX XXXX, called XXXX that day and by Monday the situation had suddenly changed. The excuse was that the underwriter had thought I was requesting to assume the loan from my husband. I am not married and I am the only owner on this home. The loan was assumable.
Many times, I explained to XXXX that delaying this is costing me and that while we wait, no one can show the home or bid for the home. So the longer they delay, the less time I have to try to get another plan in place to prevent foreclosure.
In XXXX, on the Friday before the cyber attack became news, XXXX called me stating that she can finally start to review the application this weekend. However, that XX/XX/XXXX Friday the cyber attack started and computers were down for XXXX weeks per the loan depot employee I spoke with. Thus, the application sat there for almost XXXX months with no review by the assumptions representative. It was around this time that my assumptions rep stopped calling me back. I found out later that a new assumptions representative was on the case, XXXX. I never received any notice of this change and had left messages for XXXX multiple times.
Forbearance extension and closing So my forbearance had to be extended until XX/XX/XXXX to cover these unnecessary and costly delays. I requested compensation for having to wait on them due to their inability to staff correctly or keep their computer systems safe from hackers. Loan depot denied my request. XXXX XXXX a complaint resolution specialist said that with assumptions only XXXX person is assigned the loan, where new loans have about XXXX people to assist with an original loan. My loan servicer was not motivated to complete this loan assumption. I was unable to use a different loan company and so I was at their mercy Closing disclosures with the buyers were signed on XX/XX/XXXX and closing date was set for XX/XX/XXXX and then the loan was transferred to XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, so closing was delayed again. I called and asked for the ability to close the loan on the XXXX anyway, if there was a grace period and there was nothing anyone could do. They would not give me the number to who could do something so I was stuck again. I filed the initial cfpb complaint, called lawyers, called hud and the va representative.
CREDIT REPORTING On XX/XX/XXXX, Loan depot reported me as seriously delinquent to the credit agencies even though I was in a forbearance and had been current when I was approved for loss mitigation. I had to dispute this with the credit reporting agencies and my credit score is at XXXX now. I am paying more for insurance and I am not getting a good rate on my consolidation loan.
I received a response from the dispute. Here is what is written in my credit report dispute. Please note that you say that reported me correctly and then modify the information. If you had reported me correctly, I would not have had to dispute this with the credit reporting agencies. It states the following : The information you disputed has been updated. LOAN DEPOT reports, We have verified that this item has been reported correctly. The following fields have been modified : ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, TERMS DURATION, ACTIVITY DESIGNATOR, DATE OF 1ST DELINQUENCY, and STATUS HISTORICAL ACCOUNT INFORMATION.
Everywhere I read, the forbearance protected my credit since the loan servicer stated they have to report me as current. I was waiting on them to move on this assumption application and I assumed they would not report me as delinquent especially since they were causing the delay. In addition, I had a forbearance and was in contact with them and have given them a viable plan for assumption. However, I was wrong. Loan depot reported me as 120 days past due and seriously delinquent and my credit score dropped by over XXXX points in the last year.
Data Breech In addition to all the above issues, I was sent a letter stating my sensitive personal information may have been accessed by an unauthorized third party. Loan depot offers 2 years of identity protection and credit monitoring. This was unexpected since during the transfer to XXXX in XXXX, I requested information on this data breech and was told that my data was not part of the data breech. Yet a few weeks later, I received a notice that my data was exposed. The notice was dated XX/XX/XXXX. I did not receive it until late XXXX. I had to freeze all my credit accounts and now I need review them regularly. I am closing accts to prevent use of an open acct I dont use and that is lowering my score also.

IN SUMMARY Loan depot delayed review of the loan for 3 months, then scheduled closing, signed documents and sold my loan to XXXX causing another delay. Then they report me as seriously delinquent when I have been waiting on them. My forbearance was good until XX/XX/XXXX ( see Proof of forbearance letter from loan depot ) and never lapsed.
On XX/XX/XXXX I received a letter regarding these issues and Loan depot apologized for the delays and inconvenience I experienced. That does not make up for the money that your company received due to these delays ( XXXX ). That does not make my credit better or my interest rates lower. It doesnt pay for the time and energy I have had to use to try to make you do your job This letter also stated as the forbearance was active at the time the servicing transfer occurred, the account was accurately reported to the credit agencies as current and in a forbearance. However, if you have credit reports reflecting information that you believe to be inaccurate, we respectfully request you forward legible documentation that may support your credit dispute so that we can further research your account and referred to the letter dated XX/XX/XXXX. The letter dated XX/XX/XXXX stated that after careful review of your loan, we have made the necessary adjustments for the forbearance and/or loss mitigation status in relation to the loans credit reporting.
So here are some questions I would like answered.
Did you report me as seriously delinquent or current, it seems that you are not sure and so I have attached proof ( you'd think you'd have this information )? Why would your company do this when I was waiting on your staff to review the loan assumption application?
When did XXXX XXXX start to work on the assumption application? Include dates and actions taken since you have told me she started to work on this on XXXX and she told me she did not start until XX/XX/XXXX.
What was the reason for the application to need XXXX months of review?
When did XXXX XXXX stop working as my point of contact? Why did I not receive a letter with the change in the point of contact?

Why were the buyers told they could not assume the loan in the beginning and then were told this could take 120 days. Its almost like you planned it and were trying to discourage them. It took 120 days and not because we were delaying anything.
Why did you have the buyers sign closing disclosures on XXXX and ask for them to pay the forbearance money when you were transferring them and knew this information in early XXXX?
Why did you not safeguard my information and tell me I was not part of the breach and then send a letter stating I was part of it?
Did you send XXXX my loss mitigation information?



Names and contact numbers of people in this complaint XXXX XXXX, Escalation resolution specialist for Loan depot XXXX XXXX XXXX ( This may not be the correct spelling ) XXXX XXXX the XXXX loan servicer in assumptions. XXXX XXXX XXXX, VA representative XXXX XXXX Attachments Forbearance letter proving I was in a forbearance until XXXX on page XXXX of the document Data breech letter Letters XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX Credit report dispute information-Showing the changes made to my credit report -Equifax Credit report showing over 120 days late from XXXX. Hopefully that proves the reason for the credit dispute and you will investigate further per letter dated XX/XX/XXXX.

issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue with the application process
narrative:
Subject : Urgent : Unjust and predatory practices by Village Capital Dear XXXX, I am a 100 % XXXX veteran and I am reaching out to you with deep concern and frustration regarding my ongoing experience with Village Capital, my current mortgage holder ; My situation has escalated over this past year, and I believe it is imperative that you understand the gravity of what I've been subjected to : In XXXX of XXXX, I began the process of refinancing my home. Initially, I was advised to withhold my XXXX payment, as we were assured that closing would occur within days. Unfortunately, the loan fell through, and I found myself in a precarious position. I was then introduced to XXXX, who worked tirelessly to secure a new loan for me. But, despite her efforts and multiple communications with my creditors, the loan never materialized due to unforeseen obstacles.

During this time, I reached out to Village Capital 's loss mitigation department to arrange bringing my account current. I followed through on the agreed-upon terms, yet my XX/XX/XXXX payment was inexplicably placed in " suspense '' and not honored until after 30 days, putting me in arrears through no fault of my own.

Now, a year later, in an attempt to refinance again, I was denied due to a XXXX late payment that was in reality not late at all ; This situation has continued to spiral out of control. In XX/XX/XXXX, following an audit by Village Capital, my monthly payment suddenly jumped from {$1400.00} to nearly {$1700.00}. I paid the regular amount and separately covered the {$270.00} difference. By XXXX my payment had inexplicably risen to {$1800.00}, despite no legitimate in my insurance or property taxes. In fact, both had in fact decreased, which I confirmed directly with my insurance provider and the county assessor 's office.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a call from Village Capital 's collection department demanding {$4000.00}. The following day, Village Capital called offering an in-house refinance, suggesting they would drop the discrepancy - an offer that only added to my confusion and distress. Now, despite my mortgage documents clearly stating a regular, fixed, payment of {$1400.00}, I am being asked to pay {$1800.00} plus an additional {$490.00}, with no clear or honest explanation.

It seems clear to me that Village Capital is engaging in predatory practices, exploiting the most vulnerable, including veterans like myself. My sole source of income is my VA benefits plus XXXX Social Security, which Village Capital already takes 25 % of. The stress, heartache, and sleepless nights caused by Village Capital 's actions have become overwhelming. I believe Village Capital is attempting to force me out of my home by any means necessary, and this is something I can not, and will not, stand for.

As a veteran who has served this country, I refuse to be bullied, abused or taken advantage of. This is not just about me, this is about protecting all veterans and homeowners who may be facing similar harassment. I firmly believe that Village Capital should be removed from XXXX XXXXXXXX, and I will be pursuing all avenues to ensure that justice is served.

I have compiled extensive documentation, including payment history, letters from my insurance and tax offices and all relevant correspondence with XXXX XXXX. I am prepared to share this evidence with the VA and any other relevant authorities.

This situation is intolerable, and I am seeking a resolution that not only addresses my case but also sends a clear message to XXXX XXXX and any other lenders that such treatment will not be tolerated. I urge you to take immediate action and to rectify this situation and protect those who have served our country eith honor.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response.

Sincerely

sub_product: Home equity loan or line of credit (HELOC)
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
narrative:
I have been banking with Citibank since XXXX and have had several mortgage loans and a HELOC over the years. The mortgage loan and HELOC were paid off in XX/XX/XXXX.

In XX/XX/XXXX, I applied for a {$100000.00} HELOC on my current primary residence ( in Florida ) to fund a special assessment on my condominium property. The application process was arduous ( for the amount of the loan request ). Citibank requested many additional documents, which were provided promptly. My credit details were : 1. Credit Score > XXXX 2. Loan to value less than 10 % ( Citibank appraisal of {$1.00} XXXX ).
3. No existing mortgage debt ( subject property is free and clear ).
4. Own a second home free and clear ( value of {$670.00} thousand ).
5. Income is derived from two pensions, Social Security, part-time work, and income/dividends from investment accounts.

My credit history is unblemished, I good liquidity, low debt and a good net worth.

A counteroffer was made at {$38000.00}, apparently based on the fact that my debt-to-income ratio was too high. I contacted the mortgage representative and indicated that I had {$220.00} XXXX in liquid assets and {$1.00} XXXX in an IRA with XXXX XXXX, which I felt had not been considered in their analysis of the loan request. Furthermore, I indicated that I would have to take Required Minimum Distributions from the IRA beginning in XXXX which would be approximately {$75.00} to {$80.00} XXXX annually. I also stated that funds in the IRA could be withdrawn at any time without penalty to support payment of any debt.

On XX/XX/XXXX, the HELOC request was declined for : 1 ) Insufficient income for the total obligation and 2 ) Insufficient Citi banking relationship for the loan request.

I believe that Citibank 's underwriting practices are discriminatory in that other sources of income such as withdrawals from IRA accounts whether discretionary or required are not taken into consideration, and that accommodation is not made for such potential source of income.

Subsequent to the decline of the HELOC application, Citibank has sent me two mortgage loan offers ( up to {$2000.00} off on closing costs ). This seems incongruent and is insulting.

sub_product: Home equity loan or line of credit (HELOC)
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during the application process
narrative: I applied for a home equity loan from Nationstar Mortgage in XX/XX/year>. The process was protracted and there were multiple increases in the quoted rate. I ultimately chose not to move forward with the loan application. The issue is that in XX/XX/year>, before I had agreed to the loan or signed anything other than my disclosure acknowledgments, Nationstar contacted my home insurer, XXXX, represented itself as the holder of a second, perfected mortgage note on my home, and inserted itself as the second mortgagee on my home insurance policy without my knowledge or permission. It did not call XXXX back in XXXX, after we ended the loan application process, to remove itself. It appears that Nationstar was trying to entitle itself to an insurance payout it did not deserve in the event of a catastrophic event.

My monthly home mortgage rate has been ~ {$6200.00} since we purchased our home in XX/XX/year>. Last year, our bike was stolen from the front of our house and we had a minor theft reported while travelling. Both were reported to home insurance. Because of that, my home insurance ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) declined to renew our coverage last XXXX. My mortgage servicer, US Bank, claims they tried to inform me of this change via mail. When told that I never received any notification, and upon being asked to show proof they tried to communicate with me via email or phone ( my preferred method of communication and the way they communicate with me for all other reasons ), neither they nor XXXX were able to show me any proof.

As a result of my home insurance being cancelled, US Bank then elected to sign me up for their own home
insurance plan, which was roughly 4.5x the cost of my previous plan ( {$800.00} per month, instead of {$160.00} per month ) per. BUT, they did not actually start charging me this on a monthly basis until XXXX, when my escrow was fully depleted and they conducted a new escrow analysis. XXXX Bank did not provide explanation for why they waited this long to conduct a new escrow analysis, which does not make sense since they knew my escrow would quickly be exhausted by the new plan.

In XXXX, my monthly mortgage payment suddenly jumped from ~ {$6200.00} to ~ {$7800.00}. As soon as I discovered why, I changed my insurance that day to another XXXX plan for {$220.00} a month, nearly {$600.00} cheaper than the plan US Bank forced me to sign up for. And because I didn't find out about it until XXXX, I accrued nearly XXXX XXXX in extra expenses, that US Bank is now charging me for to catch up to my old account.

US Bank acknowledges I never consented to the higher rate, and would not provide any proof it tried to contact me via email, despite acknowledging that is how I elected to receive notification. US Bank also refused to provide any reimbursement, despite no claims being filed
on my home during this period. XXXX also did not provide me any explanation as to why they did not communicate with me via email, despite using email for all other communication up to that point ( including sharing my policy with me when I first signed up ).

I find this practice highly misleading and unfair - I never knew about my insurance cancellation, no one tried to contact me via email or phone, and they signed me up for a much more expensive plan without my consent th
at cost me nearly XXXX XXXX

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue related to modification, forbearance, short sale, deed-in-lieu, bankruptcy, or foreclosure
narrative:
Follow up from last interaction : A separate letter we received from SPS last month was denying our mortgage assistance repayment program, because as SPS stated, " They could not reduce our payment enough to support repayment, even though we stated many times over the phone and through our mortgage assistance package, that we were not looking to reduce our payment, we simply want to form a repayment plan to add on to our monthly payment and repay overtime.
After that, we received another letter saying we were denied again because our financials did not support a balance being repaid over a 12 month time period.

When we asked to supply evidence of additional supplemental income they denied our request to provide it.

Our " relationship manager, XXXX '' is consistently unavailable and never returns our calls or emails. We have tried numerous times to contact your " servicing representatives '', but they also consistently tell us they can provide no additional information or help to us, only " XXXX '' who is never available. There is no one we can ever receive any help/info from and in fact, the only detailed responses we ever receive from SPS is through these complaints.

As weve stated, in these claims, and our previous claim through CFPB with XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX admitted AND provided documentation to CFPB that there was a glitch in their system, which never alerted us that we were approved for their plan, and then they immediately sold our mortgage to SPS. So we did not " miss '' our first trial payment, we were never " alerted '' that we were approved to make a payment prior to XXXX XXXX selling our mortgage to SPS.

We have worked tirelessly with both companies in order to meet their qualifications and demands, even though they are constantly changing on a monthly basis. We simply want to find a suitable agreement in order to start repaying the owed amount due to the forbearance plan.

As per the forbearance laws that were set in place from XXXX, there were to be multiple ways in order to repay the forbearance amount.

You have denied our right to use any of the other options that were meant by law to be part of the forbearance terms and conditions which should have included the following : If you have a XXXX XXXX or XXXX XXXXbacked loan, you have five post-forbearance options : Reinstatement : Youll repay all the payments you missed right away after your forbearance.

Repayment plan : Youll establish a repayment plan and pay off a portion of your missed payments each month until the entire missed amount is accounted for.

Deferral : Youll defer the missed payments until the maturity date on your loan when they become due as a non-interest-bearing balance.

Flex Modification program : Your servicer will permanently extend your loans term or lower your interest rate to lower your monthly payment.

Refinance : Youll obtain a new loan with a new interest rate and term. To qualify, youll need to be officially off the forbearance plan, have made at least three consecutive monthly payments and meet the lenders eligibility requirements.

And now unfortunately we are not eligible for refinance, because our account was illegally locked immediately
when the forbearance plan ended with XXXX XXXX and then remained locked by SPS when it was sold to them in XX/XX/XXXX. In our copy of the official " NOTE '' that was sent to us by SPS, it states that " the borrower has the right to make payments at any time ''.

Since SPS is now saying that the only option is to repay the entire past due amount of {$110000.00} over a 12 month period ( on page 1 and 2 of attached letter ), I have procured a loan recently supporting that amount allowing us to take monthly draws. According to my calculations based on the {$110000.00}, it should roughly be about XXXX XXXX plus the mortgage amount of {$2400.00} per month. narrative:
On XX/XX/2024, I received a loan estimate from Better.com for a mortgage on my property. Before receiving this estimate, I was repeatedly assured by the company 's representative, XXXX, that all costs except for Prepaids ( F ) and Initial Escrow Payment ( G ) would be covered by lender credits. Based on this information, I decided to lock in the mortgage rate with them.

However, upon reviewing the loan estimate today, I found that additional costs were included that were supposed to be covered by the lender credits, contrary to what was promised. This has caused unexpected financial concerns, as I was explicitly told that these costs would not apply.

Immediately after reviewing the estimate, I made multiple attempts to contact XXXX via phone and text messages throughout the day to clarify this discrepancy, but I have received no response. I also reached out to Better.com to escalate the issue, but I have not yet received any communication or resolution from the company.

Due to this misleading information and lack of response, I feel I have been misled about the true costs associated with this mortgage. I have requested that Better.com either adjust the loan terms to reflect the promised lender credits or cancel the loan process altogether with a full refund of my {$550.00} fee. Additionally, I seek compensation for the hard credit inquiry on my account and the time wasted due to this issue.

As of now, Better.com has not taken any steps to address or resolve my concerns.

I was contacted by Chase Bank by letter that they had determined that my condominium address is in a flood zone, and therefore, I must buy flood insurance to protect their interest in the condo unit. Oddly, they also wrote that FEMA determined my home was in a flood zone.

If I did not purchase flood insurance for them, they would do so for me and pass the costs on to me by raising my mortgage. They then wrote that if I believe I'm not in a flood zone, to contact FEMA and to request an amendment or revision to the flood map ( XXXX or XXXX ) and that I would have to pay for any costs associated with getting them to revise the map and send proof. I went to the FEMA website, entered my address and it showed that I was NOT in a flood zone as of XX/XX/year>. So I'm not sure what they are supposed to revise or amend.

I called Chase to get more information and the person I spoke to reiterated that FEMA made this determination. I point her to the FEMA website and she said she was not sure how to read the map. She asked me to contact FEMA and to go through the XXXX process.

I called FEMA and the person I spoke to said ( 1 ) that FEMA does not communicate with banks about flood map updates, and ( 2 ) Chase bank has a third party they contract with that makes the " Standard Flood Hazard Determinations '' ... or something like that. This XXXX representative then went to their maps and confirmed that my home is not in a flood zone. I asked XXXX what to do and they recommended doing both of the following actions : ( 1 ) Go through the XXXX process but that it takes 60 days ; ( 2 ) File a complaint with the regulatory body over the organization that Chase contracts with to make the determinations, and to contact the state attorney general. FEMA gave me a number to call.

I called that number, went through a long menu of option, spoke to someone who then told me that it does sound like Chase is engaged in forced-insurance. She then said the best entity to help me through this is the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau.

I decided to contact Chase one more time. I explained the situation, and relayed to them what FEMA said and that FEMA denies communicating with them directly ( contradicting what the previous Chase representative said ). This new Chase rep admits that it was another entity that makes that determination, and reiterated the only remedy available to me is to fill out the XXXX forms at my expense. She also reiterated that if I don't buy a flood insurance policy for Chase, they would do it for me, and pass the costs to me, even though it'll take 2 months before FEMA can send proof that I'm not in a flood zone. She mentioned something about offering a refund if they were wrong, but that seems to contradict aspects of what is written in the letter, particularly where there's a gap in coverage.

In short, as best as I understand it, Chase contracts with some 3rd Party to make determinations about whether a property is in a flood zone, then Chase claims that it was FEMA who made that determination, then demands homeowners to buy flood insurance to protect Chase 's interest in the property. If the homeowner doesn't comply, Chase will buy what they openly admit may not be the most affordable insurance. To comply with their demand, they give homeowners LESS than the 60 days it takes to go through the XXXX process ( which you may have to pay for ) to prove you are not in the flood zone, even though ANYONE can simply go to the FEMA website, enter their address, and see that they are not in a flood zone.

I did a quick XXXX search and it seems Chase has a history of doing this.

This is not at all fair.

Third time requesting.

My XXXX is well outside 80 % since house prices have gone up and improvements made.

I have no 30 day lates in the past 12 months and no 60 day lates in the last 24 months.

This defeats PMI, I am within the HPA requirements.

I need the instructions to remove PMI, a list of companies to use to purchase an official appraisal. NewRez/Shellpoint has not supplied this information.

Please supply this information - 3rd time requesting.

This is nothing more than a delay tactic, trying to get to the point where they can foreclose on our home.


NOTICE : The following reflects my honest opinions about Shellpoint Mortgage Inc. 's products, services, or conduct, as protected by the Consumer Review Fairness Act passed unanimously by Congress to protect peoples ability to share in any forum their honest opinions and experiences.

I have spoken to someone about my experiences with you and Shellpoint regarding my efforts to secure a loan forbearance. He asked me a few questions about my experience and I responded. If I'm not mistaken, that was the THIRD technique you had a asked me to try. We apologize for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your patience.

I found the error message to be interesting timing. And, finally, of course, I have electronic faxed this email to the fax number you provided. I honestly hope you will get at least XXXX of those other communication attempts I have made just to get this email to you.

The ONLY modifications to my email to the person with whom I am communicating are any references by me that might reveal the identity of the person I am communicating with. And, of course, I did not provide copies of the XXXX emails exchanged between you and me because you, like me, have the originals of those. There were others, but I believe the ones I referenced are most relevant to this issue.

Please forgive me for the occasional use of profanity in my response to him. Please be assured I would not use such prifanity with you, as it woud not be appropriate in business correspondence, but this is the text of that email : Thanks for getting back to me.

The answers to your questions are below. But before you review my answers, please look at the attachment labelled XXXX XXXX Shellpoint XXXX, which we just received today regarding our request to Shellpoint for a forbearance. Sounds to me like they are getting very close to foreclosure.

Here are the answers to your questions : XXXX. Here is an overall count of emails between XXXX XXXX '' XXXX '' and me, between XXXX, the day after I received her first phone call, through XXXX : Received XXXX Sent XXXX Of course, I would be more than happy to forward you a copy of ALL emails exchanged. I did not do that yet, because I dont want to overwhelm you with even more paperwork.

XXXX. Yes. Completed once and sent XXXX different ways, including ALL the documents she requested, copies of which are attached to this email. In each and every case, she claimed she had not received them, then suggested yet another technique to use to send them. I followed her instructions PRECISELY as to how they should be sent, where they should be sent, etc., etc., etc. As you may recall, I have been a geek for well over 50 years, so I know how to do this. And EVERY time, she said I MUST be doing something wrong, which, of course, is total BS there is absolutely nothing I can do on my end to cause problems it could ONLY be a problem on her end.

a. Attached to email replying to her when she originally asked for the information.

b. Attached to a second email reply to her, all in PDF format, since she notified me that was the only format her system would accept. She did not mention this in her first email.

c. Attached to another email address, since she informed me all information, they requested had to be sent to that email address, or they couldnt view them.

i. Spoke to another person in her office/on her team, who informed me the email just sent to the specific email address had been received and it would take a day or XXXX for it to be sent from that email address internally to XXXX XXXX.

d. XXXX XXXX XXXX she had not received anything from their internal email distribution system and told me I had to fax them to a specific fax number. Since we are in the process of moving ( the downsizing effort I described ), our fax machine was in storage, so I signed up for an account with XXXX, a highly respected electronic faxing service. I faxed all the requested information to that number and sent the log of the successful faxes to XXXX XXXX.

e. This is the text of the last email I sent her, on XXXX, regarding all her document requests and my responses. As you can tell, I was a bit upset : XXXX XXXX...

I have lost track of how many documents you have requested that I have sent to you PRECISELY FOLLOWING YOUR INSTRUCTIONS, which you never get. And DON'T tell me it's somehow my fault. That is PATENTLY ABSURD. There is NOTHING I can do on my side that would affect your not receiving them. The information my attorney sent me was not in detail however. It only had 2 different options with the Effective Date, New Maturity Date, New Interest Rate and New Total Payment. sub_issue: Closing disclosure or other related disclosures
narrative:
I requested a refund of the appraisal fee that I was charged during a recent mortgage application process with Residential Mortgage Services. I believe the fee was unjustly charged due to the mishandling of my loan application and the failure to provide the required documentation and information, as outlined below. XXXX XXXX was my lender and XXXX XXXX is the manager for the branch. Ive been upfront from the beginning on my employment contracts, and stating that Im an employee contracted to work during the school year. I had a pre approval already from XXXX XXXX  but went with residential mortgage because of a grant program offered. After residential mortgage failed, to finance me I now own the home through a XXXX XXXX loan. Isnt that ironic.
Background : The appraisal fee of {$700.00} was requested and paid on XX/XX/2024, under the assumption that all preliminary underwriting checks had been completed and that the loan was likely to proceed. However, it was not until XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX days after the appraisal fee was paidthat Residential Mortgage asked for my school contracts and discovered that my employment is for 9 months out of the year, which ultimately led to the loan being denied.

Residential Mortgage failed to conduct proper due diligence before requesting the appraisal fee. Information regarding my employment contract should have been reviewed prior to ordering the appraisal. If this critical information had been considered earlier, it would have been clear that the loan could not be approved under their criteria, and the appraisal fee would not have been necessary.
Issues : XXXX. Mishandling of Loan Application : Despite my upfront disclosure regarding my employment status, Residential Mortgage Services proceeded with ordering the appraisal immediately after receiving my school contracts, which confirmed my nine-month work schedule. Instead of pausing the appraisal order to reassess the situation, Residential Mortgage Services moved forward, leading to a situation where I was later informed that my loan could not be approved.
XXXX. Lack of Formal Denial : The only communication I received from XXXX XXXX was an email stating, Per underwriting, we arent going to be able to approve this loan. I never received an official denial letter, which I believe is required by law under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( ECOA ). This act requires lenders to provide a list of the main reasons for denying a loan or inform the applicant of their right to request this information. When I asked for a denial letter, I was not provided with an official one.
XXXX. Misleading Guidance : After receiving the vague denial statement from XXXX XXXX, I was advised to pay down debt and try again next year. Had I followed this guidance, I would have lost the opportunity to purchase the home I now own, thanks to securing financing with another lender. This further demonstrates the flawed handling of my application by Residential Mortgage Services.
Request : Given these circumstances, I request a full refund of the appraisal fee. I believe it is only fair, considering the appraisal was ordered prematurely and without proper consideration of the underwriting issues that were present at the time.

We became very concerned about fraud and identity theft, because it appeared the XXXX had mixed up our records with those from a different customer. Where else could the false insurer and expiration data come from?

For this latest false claim by the Servicer ( letter # XXXX ), we sent the Servicer letter # XXXX dated XX/XX/24 attached. Considering the past and current inaccurate Servicer recordkeeping, our letter reasonably requested that the servicer provide us with a summary of past entries, changes and data sources for the insurance information they had entered on our account records. Only by seeing this infromation can we compare it to insurance information sent to the servicer by agents and insurers. Only by doing this can we get evidence of the accuracy or ineptitude of the servicer 's recordkeeping.

Subsequently, we received the servicer 's FORM LETTER # XXXX dated XX/XX/24, indicating they now purportedly had accurate insurance information even though ( a ) they had accurate information it all along until they mixed up our records with those of other customers and ( b ) THEY DID NOT DISCLOSE WHAT INFORMATION THEY NOW HAVE so we have no way of knowing just WHAT their records show now.

The servicing provided on this mortgage loan - as it relates to insurance monitoring- is sloppy at best. At worst, it puts customer privacy at risk because customer data is not accurately updated and maintained.

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue related to modification, forbearance, short sale, deed-in-lieu, bankruptcy, or foreclosure
narrative:
I have been traveling a lot for work. While I was abroad for work, my bank I had been paying my bills from, closed the account for security because I used the connected debit card abroad, and physically mailed me cashier checks. Checks I could not access as I was abroad for work. Payments got missed. Planet Home offered loan restructuring. I had to make three equal payments at the new price. When I had successfully made those payments, they sent me the paperwork to sign, but I was not home. So I asked them prepare and email me a copy of that paperwork. They said I had a very small window ( 24-48 hours ) to sign and have it in a prepaid fedex label.
I complied with all of these.
I called to make my payment in XXXX, and they told me a payment was not due that month as the loan had been restructured. This was roughly the twentieth of XXXX, long after the delivery of the documents.
In XXXX I called to make my monthly payment, they said I owed XXXX dollars, more or less.
Upon their explanation, they said that my paper was not valid because I used a digital notary service. Which was made legal by senate bill 275. A horrible technicality.
They said they resent the documents but I didn't respond in time so the restructuring was no longer an option. I said I had check my ring door bell footage and no such delivery had been made, and I also stated it wasn't fair because I had requested that email be the main source of communication, as my work requires my constant traveling. The representative put me on hold. When they came back on the line, they said that they would be preparing the documents for me again, and emailing them to me, as I did in fact request to be sent and corresponded with via email. They said that I would receive it in one week which was three weeks ago. I have not heard anything, and I feel that I am being unfairly treated in regards to my home. I want to resolve this issue, but that are not cooperating. I do not want to lose my home because they are not doing as they say.
I also have the call recorded where they said they would email me the documents.
